                 // Can define opaque types as this is only reachable with\n                                // `generic_const_exprs`\n                                DefineOpaqueTypes::Yes,\n                                c1,\n                                c2,\n                            ) {\n                                Ok(inf_ok) => ProcessResult::Changed(mk_pending(\n                                    obligation,\n                                    inf_ok.into_obligations(),\n                                )),\n                                Err(err) => {\n                                    ProcessResult::Error(FulfillmentErrorCode::ConstEquate(\n                                        ExpectedFound::new(c1, c2),\n                                        err,\n                                    ))\n                                }\n                            }\n                        }\n                        (Err(EvaluateConstErr::InvalidConstParamTy(e)), _)\n                        | (_, Err(EvaluateConstErr::InvalidConstParamTy(e))) => {\n                            ProcessResult::Error(FulfillmentErrorCode::Select(\n                                SelectionError::NotConstEvaluatable(NotConstEvaluatable::Error(e)),\n                            ))\n                        }\n                        (Err(EvaluateConstErr::EvaluationFailure(e)), _)\n                        | (_, Err(EvaluateConstErr::EvaluationFailure(e))) => {\n                            ProcessResult::Error(FulfillmentErrorCode::Select(\n                                SelectionError::NotConstEvaluatable(NotConstEvaluatable::Error(e)),\n                            ))\n                        }\n                        (Err(EvaluateConstErr::HasGenericsOrInfers), _)\n                        | (_, Err(EvaluateConstErr::HasGenericsOrInfers)) => {\n                            if c1.has_non_region_infer() || c2.has_non_region_infer() {\n                                ProcessResult::Unchanged\n                            } else {\n                                // Two different constants using generic parameters ~> error.\n                                let expected_found = ExpectedFound::new(c1, c2);\n                                ProcessResult::Error(FulfillmentErrorCode::ConstEquate(\n                                    expected_found,\n }\n                ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::UnstableFeature(symbol)) => {\n                    if may_use_unstable_feature(self.selcx.infcx, obligation.param_env, symbol) {\n                        ProcessResult::Changed(Default::default())\n                    } else {\n                        ProcessResult::Unchanged\n                    }\n                }\n            },\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[inline(never)]\n    fn process_backedge<'c, I>(\n        &mut self,\n        cycle: I,\n        _marker: PhantomData<&'c PendingPredicateObligation<'tcx>>,\n    ) -> Result<(), FulfillmentErrorCode<'tcx>>\n    where\n        I: Clone + Iterator<Item = &'c PendingPredicateObligation<'tcx>>,\n    {\n        if self.selcx.coinductive_match(cycle.clone().map(|s| skip(self, obligation, stalled_on))]\n    fn process_trait_obligation(\n        &mut self,\n        obligation: &PredicateObligation<'tcx>,\n        trait_obligation: PolyTraitObligation<'tcx>,\n        stalled_on: &mut Vec<TyOrConstInferVar>,\n    ) -> ProcessResult<PendingPredicateObligation<'tcx>, FulfillmentErrorCode<'tcx>> {\n        let infcx = self.selcx.infcx;\n        if obligation.predicate.is_global() && !matches!(infcx.typing_mode(), TypingMode::Coherence)\n        {\n            // no type variables present, can use evaluation for better caching.\n            // holds\",\n                    obligation.recursion_depth\n                );\n                return ProcessResult::Changed(Default::default());\n            }\n        }\n\n        match    ProcessResult::Changed(mk_pending(obligation, impl_source.nested_obligations()))\n            }\n            Ok(None) => {\n                debug!(\"selecting trait at depth {} yielded Ok(None)\", TypingMode::Coherence)\n        {\n            // no type variables present, can use evaluation for better caching.\n            // FIXME: consider caching errors too.\n            if infcx.predicate_must_hold_considering_regions(obligation) {\n                if let Some(key) = ProjectionCacheKey::from_poly_projection_obligation(\n                    &mut self.selcx,\n                    &project_obligation,\n                ) {\n                    // If `predicate_must_hold_considering_regions` succeeds, then we've\n                    // evaluated all sub-obligations. We can therefore mark the 'root'\n                    // obligation as complete, and skip evaluating sub-obligations.\n                    infcx\n                        .inner\n ProcessResult::Changed(Default::default());\n            } else {\n                debug!(\"Does NOT hold: {:?}\", obligation);\n            }\n        }\n\n        match project::poly_project_and_unify_term(&mut self.selcx, &project_obligation) {\n            ProjectAndUnifyResult::Holds(os) => ProcessResult::Changed(mk_pending(obligation, os)),\n            ProjectAndUnifyResult::FailedNormalization => {\n                stalled_on.clear();\n                stalled_on.extend(args_infer_vars(\n                    &self.selcx,\n                    project_obligation.predicate.map_bound(|pred| pred.projection_term.args),\n                ));\n                ProcessResult::Unchanged\n            }\n            // Let the caller handle the recursion\n            ProjectAndUnifyResult::Recursive => {\n                let mut obligations = PredicateObligations::with_capacity(1);\n                obligations.push(project_obligation.with(tcx, project_obligation.predicate));\n\n                ProcessResult::Changed(mk_pending(obligation, obligations))\n            }\n            ProjectAndUnifyResult::MismatchedProjectionTypes(e) => {\n                ProcessResult::Error(FulfillmentErrorCode::Project(e))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn process_host_obligation(\n ProcessResult::Changed(mk_pending(obligation, nested)),\n            Err(effects::EvaluationFailure::Ambiguous) => {\n                stalled_on.clear();\n ProcessResult::Error(FulfillmentErrorCode::Select(SelectionError::Unimplemented))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Returns the set of inference variables contained in `args`.\nfn args_infer_vars<'tcx>(\n    selcx: &SelectionContext<'_, 'tcx>,\n    args: ty::Binder<'tcx, GenericArgsRef<'tcx>>,\n) -> impl Iterator<Item = TyOrConstInferVar> {\n    selcx\n        .infcx\n        .resolve_vars_if_possible(args)\n        .skip_binder() // ok because this check doesn't care about regions\n        .iter()\n        .filter(|arg| arg.has_non_region_infer())\n        .flat_map(|arg|          walker.skip_current_subtree();\n                }\n            }\n            walker.visited.into_iter()\n        })\n        .filter_map(TyOrConstInferVar::maybe_from_generic_arg)\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub struct OldSolverError<'tcx>(\n    Error<PendingPredicateObligation<'tcx>, {\n    let suggested_limit = match tcx.recursion_limit() {\n        Limit(0) => Limit(2),\n        limit => limit * 2,\n    };\n    err.help(format!(\n        \"consider increasing the recursion limit We\n    /// halt compilation unconditionally because it is important that\n    /// overflows never be masked -- they basically represent computations\n    /// whose result could not be truly determined and thus we can't say\n    /// if the program type checks or not -- and they are unusual\n    /// occurrences in any case.\n    pub fn report_overflow_error(\n        &self,\n        cause: OverflowCause<'tcx>,\n        span: Span,\n        suggest_increasing_limit: bool,\n        mutate: impl FnOnce(&mut Diag<'_>),\n    ) -> ! {\n        let mut err = self.build_overflow_error(cause, span: Span,\n        suggest_increasing_limit: bool,\n    ) -> Diag<'a> {\n        fn with_short_path<'tcx, T>(tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>, value: T) -> String\n        where\n            T: fmt::Display + Print<'tcx, FmtPrinter<'tcx, 'tcx>>,\n        {\n            let s = value.to_string();\n            if s.len() > 50 {\n                // We don't need to save the type to a file, we will be talking FmtPrinter::new_with_limit(tcx, Namespace::TypeNS, Limit(6));\n                value.print(&mut p).unwrap();\n                p.into_buffer()\n            } else {\n                s\n            }\n This is equivalent to `report_overflow_obligation` except\n    /// that we can give a more helpful error message (and, in particular,\n    /// we do not suggest increasing the overflow limit, which is not\n    /// going to help).\n    pub fn report_overflow_obligation_cycle(&self, cycle: &[PredicateObligation<'tcx>]) -> ! {\n        let cycle = self.resolve_vars_if_possible(cycle.to_owned());\n        assert!(!cycle.is_empty());\n\n        debug!(?cycle, \"report_overflow_error_cycle\");\n\n        // The 'deepest' obligation is most likely to have a useful\n        // cause 'backtrace'\n        self.report_overflow_obligation(\n            cycle.iter().max_by_key(|p| p.recursion_depth).unwrap(),\n            false,\n        );\n    }\n\n    pub fn report_overflow_no_abort(\n        &self,\n        obligation: PredicateObligation<'tcx>,\n        suggest_increasing_limit: bool,\n    ) -> ErrorGuaranteed {\n        let obligation = self.resolve_vars_if_possible(obligation);\n        let mut err = self.build_overflow_error(\n            OverflowCause::TraitSolver(obligation.predicate),\n            obligation.cause.span,\n            suggest_increasing_limit,\n        );\n        self.note_obligation_cause(&mut err, &obligation);\n        err.emit()\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":90,"oldLines":13,"newStart":90,"newLines":13,"lines":["             OverflowCause::TraitSolver(predicate) => {","                 let predicate = self.resolve_vars_if_possible(predicate);","                 match predicate.kind().skip_binder() {","-                    ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypePredicate { a, b, a_is_expected: _ })","+                    ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation { sub_ty, super_ty, a_is_expected: _ })","                     | ty::PredicateKind::Coerce(ty::CoercePredicate { a, b }) => {","                         struct_span_code_err!(","                             self.dcx(),","                             span,","                             E0275,","-                            \"overflow assigning `{a}` to `{b}`\",","+                            \"overflow assigning `{sub_ty}` to `{super_ty}`\",","                         )","                     }","                     _ => {"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

compute_applicable_impls_for_diagnostics<'tcx>(\n    infcx: &InferCtxt<'tcx>,\n    obligation: &PolyTraitObligation<'tcx>,\n) -> Vec<CandidateSource> {\n    let tcx = infcx.tcx;\n    let param_env = obligation.param_env;\n\n    let predicate_polarity = obligation.predicate.skip_binder().polarity;\n\n    let impl_may_apply = |impl_def_id| {\n        let ocx = ObligationCtxt::new(infcx);\n        infcx.enter_forall(obligation.predicate, |placeholder_obligation| {\n            let obligation_trait_ref = ocx.normalize(\n                &ObligationCause::dummy(),\n                param_env,\n                placeholder_obligation.trait_ref,\n            );\n\n            let impl_args = infcx.fresh_args_for_item(DUMMY_SP, impl_def_id);\n            let impl_trait_ref = tcx.impl_trait_ref(impl_def_id).instantiate(tcx, impl_args);\n            let impl_trait_ref =\n                ocx.normalize(&ObligationCause::dummy(), param_env, impl_trait_ref);\n\n            if let Err(_) =\n                ocx.eq(&ObligationCause::dummy(), param_env, obligation_trait_ref, impl_trait_ref)\n            {\n                return false;\n            }\n\n            let (ty::ImplPolarity::Positive, ty::PredicatePolarity::Positive)\n                | (ty::ImplPolarity::Negative, ty::PredicatePolarity::Negative) => {}\n                _ => return false,\n            }\n\n            let obligations = tcx\n                .predicates_of(impl_def_id)\n                .instantiate(tcx, impl_args)\n                .into_iter()\n                .map(|(predicate, _)| {\n                    Obligation::new(tcx, ObligationCause::dummy(), param_env, predicate)\n                })\n                // Kinda hacky, but let's just throw away obligations that overflow.\n                // This may reduce the accuracy of this check (if the obligation guides\n                // inference or it actually resulted in error after others are processed)\n                // ... but this is diagnostics code.\n                .filter(|obligation| {\n                    infcx.next_trait_solver() || infcx.evaluate_obligation(obligation).is_ok()\n                });\n ty::PolyTraitPredicate<'tcx>| {\n        let ocx = ObligationCtxt::new(infcx);\n        infcx.enter_forall(obligation.predicate, |placeholder_obligation| {\n            let obligation_trait_ref = &ObligationCause::dummy(),\n                param_env,\n                obligation_trait_ref,\n                param_env_trait_ref,\n            ) {\n                return false;\n            }\n\n            ocx.try_evaluate_obligations().is_empty()\n        })\n    };\n\n    let mut ambiguities = Vec::new();\n\n    tcx.for_each_relevant_impl(\n        obligation.predicate.def_id(),\n        obligation.predicate.skip_binder().trait_ref.self_ty(),\n        |impl_def_id| {\n            if infcx.probe(|_| impl_may_apply(impl_def_id)) {\n                ambiguities.push(CandidateSource::DefId(impl_def_id))\n            }\n        },\n    );\n\n    // If our `body_id` has been set (and isn't just from a dummy obligation cause),\n    // then try to look for a param-env clause that would apply. The way we compute\n    // this is somewhat manual, since we need the spans, so we elaborate this directly\n    // from `predicates_of` rather than actually looking at the param-env which\n    // otherwise would be more appropriate.\n    let body_id = obligation.cause.body_id;\n    if body_id != CRATE_DEF_ID {\n        let predicates = tcx.predicates_of(body_id.to_def_id()).instantiate_identity(tcx);\n        for (pred, span) in elaborate(tcx, predicates.into_iter()) {\n            let kind = pred.kind();\n            if let ty::ClauseKind::Trait(trait_pred) = kind.skip_binder()\n                && param_env_candidate_may_apply(kind.rebind(trait_pred))\n            {\n                if kind.rebind(trait_pred.trait_ref)\n                    == ty::Binder::dummy(ty::TraitRef::identity(tcx, trait_pred.def_id()))\n                {\n                    ambiguities.push(CandidateSource::ParamEnv(tcx.def_span(trait_pred.def_id())))\n                } else {\n                    ambiguities.push(CandidateSource::ParamEnv(span))\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    ambiguities\n}\n\nimpl<'a, 'tcx> TypeErrCtxt<'a, 'tcx> {\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"debug\")]\n    pub(super) fn maybe_report_ambiguity(\n        &self,\n        obligation: &PredicateObligation<'tcx>,\n    ) -> ErrorGuaranteed {\n        // Unable to successfully determine, probably means\n        // insufficient type information, but could mean\n        // ambiguous impls. The latter *ought* to be a\n        // coherence violation, so we don't report it here.\n\n        let predicate = self.resolve_vars_if_possible(obligation.predicate);\n        let span = obligation.cause.span;\n        let mut long_ty_path = None;\n\n        debug!(?predicate, obligation.cause.code = ?obligation.cause.code());\n\n        // Ambiguity errors are often caused as fallout from earlier errors.\n        // We ignore them if this `infcx` is tainted in some cases below.\n\n        let bound_predicate = predicate.kind();\n        let mut err = match bound_predicate.skip_binder() {\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::Trait(data)) => {\n                let trait_pred = bound_predicate.rebind(data);\n                debug!(?trait_pred);\n\n                if let Err(e) = predicate.error_reported() {\n                    return e;\n                }\n\n                if let Err(guar) = self.tcx.ensure_ok().coherent_trait(trait_pred.def_id()) {\n                    // Avoid bogus \"type annotations needed `Foo: Bar`\" errors on `impl Bar for Foo` in case\n                    // other `Foo` impls are incoherent.\n                    return guar;\n                }\n\n                // This is kind of a hack: it frequently happens that some earlier\n                // error prevents types from being fully inferred, and then we get\n                // a bunch of uninteresting errors saying something like \"<generic\n                // #0> doesn't implement Sized\". It may even be true that we\n                // could just skip over all checks where the self-ty is an\n                // inference variable, but I was afraid that there might be an\n                // inference variable created, registered as an obligation, and\n                // then never forced by writeback, and hence by skipping here we'd\n                // be ignoring the fact that we don't KNOW the type works\n                // out. Though even that would probably be harmless, given that\n                // we're only talking about builtin traits, which are known to be\n                // inhabited. We used to check for `self.tainted_by_errors()` to\n                // avoid inundating the user with unnecessary errors, but we now\n                // check upstream for type errors and don't add the obligations to\n                // begin with in those cases.\n                if matches!(\n                    self.tcx.as_lang_item(trait_pred.def_id()),\n                    Some(LangItem::Sized | LangItem::MetaSized)\n                ) {\n                    return match self.tainted_by_errors() {\n                        None => self\n                            .emit_inference_failure_err(\n                                obligation.cause.body_id,\n                                span,\n                                trait_pred.self_ty().skip_binder().into(),\n                                TypeAnnotationNeeded::E0282,\n                                false,\n                            )\n                            .emit(),\n                        Some(e) => e,\n                    };\n                }\n\n                // Typically, this ambiguity should only happen if\n                // there are unresolved type inference variables\n                // (otherwise it would suggest a coherence\n                // failure). But given #21974 that is not necessarily\n                // the case -- we can have multiple where clauses that\n                // are only distinguished by a region, which results\n                // in an ambiguity even when all types are fully\n                // known, since we don't dispatch based on region\n                // relationships.\n\n                // Pick the first generic parameter that still contains inference variables as the one\n                // we're going to emit an error for. If there are none (see above), fall back to\n                // a more general error.\n                let term = data\n                    .trait_ref\n                    .args\n                    .iter()\n                    .filter_map(ty::GenericArg::as_term)\n                    .find(|s| s.has_non_region_infer());\n\n                let mut err = if let Some(term) = term {\n                    self.emit_inference_failure_err(\n                        obligation.cause.body_id,\n                        span,\n                        term,\n                        TypeAnnotationNeeded::E0283,\n                        true,\n                    )\n                } else {\n                    struct_span_code_err!(\n                        self.dcx(),\n                        span,\n                        E0283,\n                        \"type annotations needed: cannot satisfy `{}`\",\n                        self.tcx.short_string(predicate, &mut long_ty_path),\n                    )\n                    .with_long_ty_path(long_ty_path)\n                };\n\n                let mut ambiguities = compute_applicable_impls_for_diagnostics(\n                    self.infcx,\n                    &obligation.with(self.tcx, trait_pred),\n                );\n                let has_non_region_infer = trait_pred\n                    .skip_binder()\n                    .trait_ref\n                    .args\n                    .types()\n                    .any(|t| !t.is_ty_or_numeric_infer());\n                // It doesn't make sense to talk about applicable impls if there are more than a\n                // handful of them. If there are a lot of them, but only a few of them have no type\n                // params, we only show those, as they are more likely to be useful/intended.\n                if ambiguities.len() > 5 {\n                    let infcx = self.infcx;\n                    if !ambiguities.iter().all(|option| match option {\n                        CandidateSource::DefId(did) => infcx.tcx.generics_of(*did).count() == 0,\n                        CandidateSource::ParamEnv(_) => true,\n                    }) {\n                        // If not all are blanket impls, we filter blanked impls out.\n                        ambiguities.retain(|option| match option {\n                            CandidateSource::DefId(did) => infcx.tcx.generics_of(*did).count() == 0,\n                            CandidateSource::ParamEnv(_) => true,\n                        });\n                    }\n                }\n                if ambiguities.len() > 1 && ambiguities.len() < 10 && has_non_region_infer {\n                    if let Some(e) = self.tainted_by_errors()\n                        && term.is_none()\n                    {\n                        // If `arg.is_none()`, then this is probably two param-env\n                        // candidates or impl candidates that are equal modulo lifetimes.\n                        // Therefore, if we've already emitted an error, just skip this\n                        // one, since it's not particularly actionable.\n                        err.cancel();\n                        return e;\n                    }\n                    self.annotate_source_of_ambiguity(&mut err, &ambiguities, predicate);\n                } else {\n                    if let Some(e) = self.tainted_by_errors() {\n                        err.cancel();\n                        return e;\n                    }\n                    let pred = self.tcx.short_string(predicate, &mut err.long_ty_path());\n          if impl_candidates.len() < 40 {\n                        self.report_similar_impl_candidates(\n                            impl_candidates.as_slice(),\n                            trait_pred,\n                            obligation.cause.body_id,\n                            &mut err,\n                            false,\n                            obligation.param_env,\n                        );\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if let ObligationCauseCode::WhereClause(def_id, _)\n                | ObligationCauseCode::WhereClauseInExpr(def_id, ..) = *obligation.cause.code()\n                {\n                    self.suggest_fully_qualified_path(&mut err, def_id, span, trait_pred.def_id());\n                }\n\n                if ty::AssocKind::Type { .. } => (\"refer to the\", \"type\"),\n                        };\n\n                        // Replace the more general E0283 with a more specific error\n     }\n\n                        err.span_label(span, format!(\"cannot {verb} associated {noun} of trait\"));\n\n                        let trait_impls =          }\n                },\n                CandidateSource::ParamEnv(span) => {\n                    has_param_env = true;\n                    spans.push(*span);\n                }\n post.dedup();\n\n        if self.tainted_by_errors().is_some()\n            && (crate_names.len() == 1\n                && spans.len() == 0\n                && [\"`core`\", \"`alloc`\", \"`std`\"].contains(&crate_names[0].as_str())\n                || predicate.visit_with(&mut HasNumericInferVisitor).is_break())\n        {\n            // Avoid complaining about other inference issues for expressions like\n            // `42 >> 1`, where the types are still `{integer}`, but we want to\n            // Do we need `trait_ref.skip_binder().self_ty().is_numeric() &&` too?\n            // NOTE(eddyb) this was `.cancel()`, but `err`\n            // is borrowed, so we can't fully defuse it.\n            err.downgrade_to_delayed_bug();\n            return;\n        }\n\n err.note(format!(\"and another `impl` found in the `{}` crate{}\", crates[0], post,));\n            }\n            _ => {\n                let span: MultiSpan = spans.into();\n                err.span_note(span, msg);\n                err.note(format!(\n                    \"and more `impl`s found in the following crates: {}{}\",\n                    crate_names.join(\", \"),\n The next-generation trait solver, currently still WIP.\n//!\n//! As a user of rust, you can use `-Znext-solver` to enable the new trait solver.\n//!\n//! As a developer of rustc, you shouldn't be using the new trait\n//! solver without asking the trait-system-refactor-initiative, but it can\n//! be enabled with `InferCtxtBuilder::with_next_trait_solver`. self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self))]\n    fn compute_region_outlives_goal(\n        &mut self,\n self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS)\n                }\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Infer(_) => {\n                self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS)\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Placeholder(_) | ty::ConstKind::Value(_) | ty::ConstKind::Error(_) => {\n          ty::ConstKind::Param(_) | ty::ConstKind::Bound(_, _) | ty::ConstKind::Expr(_) => {\n                panic!(\"unexpected const kind: {:?}\", ct)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n EvalCtxt<'_, D>\nwhere\n    D: SolverDelegate<Interner = I>,\n    I: Interner,\n{\n    /// Try to merge multiple possible ways to prove a goal, if that is not possible returns `None`.\n    ///\n    /// In this case we tend to flounder and return ambiguity by calling `[EvalCtxt::flounder]`.\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self), ret)]\n    fn try_merge_candidates(\n        &mut self,\n        candidates: &[Candidate<I>],\n    ) -> Option<(CanonicalResponse<I>, MergeCandidateInfo)> {\n        if candidates.is_empty() {\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        let always_applicable = candidates.iter().enumerate().find(|(_, candidate)| {\n            candidate.result.value.certainty == Certainty::Yes\n                && has_no_inference_or_external_constraints(candidate.result)\n        });\n        if let Some((i, c)) = always_applicable {\n            return Some((c.result, MergeCandidateInfo::AlwaysApplicable(i)));\n        }\n\n        let one: CanonicalResponse<I> = candidates[0].result;\n        if candidates[1..].iter().all(|candidate| candidate.result == one) {\n            return Some((one, MergeCandidateInfo::EqualResponse));\n        }\n\n        None\n    }\n\n    fn bail_with_ambiguity(&mut self, candidates: &[Candidate<I>]) -> CanonicalResponse<I> {\n        debug_assert!(candidates.len() > 1);\n        let (cause, opaque_types_jank) = candidates.iter().fold(\n            (MaybeCause::Ambiguity, OpaqueTypesJank::AllGood),\n            |(c, jank), candidates| {\n                // We pull down the certainty of `Certainty::Yes` to ambiguity when combining\n                // these responses, b/c we're combining more than one response and this we\n                // don't know which one applies.\n                match candidates.result.value.certainty {\n                    Certainty::Yes => (c, jank),\n                    Certainty::Maybe { cause, opaque_types_jank } => {\n                        (c.or(cause), jank.or(opaque_types_jank))\n                    }\n                }\n            },\n        );\n        self.make_ambiguous_response_no_constraints(cause, opaque_types_jank)\n    }\n\n    /// If we fail to merge responses we flounder and return overflow or ambiguity.\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self), ret)]\n    fn flounder(&mut self, candidates: &[Candidate<I>]) -> QueryResult<I> {\n        if candidates.is_empty() {\n            return Err(NoSolution);\n        } else {\n            Ok(self.bail_with_ambiguity(candidates))\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Normalize a type for when it is structurally matched on.\n    ///\n    /// This function is necessary in nearly all cases before structurally_normalize_term(\n        &mut self,\n        param_env: I::ParamEnv,\n        term: I::Term,\n    ) -> Result<I::Term, NoSolution> {\n        if let Some(_) = term.to_alias_term() {\n            let normalized_term = self.next_term_infer_of_kind(term);\n            let alias_relate_goal = Goal::new(\n                self.cx(),\n                param_env,\n                ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                    term,\n                    normalized_term,\n                    ty::AliasRelationDirection::Equate,\n                ),\n            );\n            // We normalize the self type to be able to relate it with\n            // types from candidates.\n            self.add_goal(GoalSource::TypeRelating, alias_relate_goal);\n            self.try_evaluate_added_goals()?;\n            Ok(self.resolve_vars_if_possible(normalized_term))\n        } else {\n            Ok(term)\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn opaque_type_is_rigid(&self, def_id: I::DefId) -> bool {\n        match self.typing_mode() {\n            // Opaques are never rigid outside of analysis mode.\n            TypingMode::Coherence | TypingMode::PostAnalysis => false,\n            // During analysis, opaques are rigid unless they may be defined by\n            // the current body.\n            TypingMode::Analysis { defining_opaque_types_and_generators: non_rigid_opaques }\n            | TypingMode::Borrowck { defining_opaque_types: non_rigid_opaques }\n            | TypingMode::PostBorrowckAnalysis { defined_opaque_types: non_rigid_opaques } => {\n                !def_id.as_local().is_some_and(|def_id| non_rigid_opaques.contains(&def_id))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// The result of evaluating a goal.\n#[derive_where(Debug; I: Interner)]\npub struct GoalEvaluation<I: Interner> {\n    /// The goal we've evaluated. This is the input goal, but potentially with its\n    /// inference variables resolved. This never applies any inference constraints\n    /// from evaluating the goal.\n    ///\n    /// We rely on this to check whether root goals in HIR typeck had an unresolved\n    /// type inference variable in the input. We must not resolve this after evaluating\n    /// the goal as even if the inference variable has been resolved by evaluating the\n    /// goal itself, this goal may still end up failing due to region uniquification\n    /// later on.\n    ///\n    /// This is used as a minor optimization to avoid re-resolving inference variables\n    /// when reevaluating ambiguous goals. E.g. if we've got a goal `?x: Trait` with `?x`\n    /// already being constrained to `Vec<?y>`, then the first evaluation resolves it to\n    /// `Vec<?y>: Trait`. If this goal is still ambiguous and we later resolve `?y` to `u32`,\n    /// then reevaluating this goal now only needs to resolve `?y` while it would otherwise\n    /// have to resolve both `?x` and `?y`,\n    pub goal: Goal<I, I::Predicate>,\n    pub certainty: Certainty,\n    pub has_changed: HasChanged,\n    /// If the [`Certainty`] was `Maybe`, then keep track of whether the goal has changed\n    /// before rerunning it.\n    pub stalled_on: Option<GoalStalledOn<I>>,\n}\n\n/// The conditions that must change for a goal to warrant\n#[derive_where(Clone, Debug; I: Interner)]\npub struct GoalStalledOn<I: Interner> {\n    pub num_opaques: usize,\n    pub stalled_vars: Vec<I::GenericArg>,\n    pub sub_roots: Vec<TyVid>,\n    /// The certainty that will be returned on subsequent evaluations if this\n    /// goal remains stalled.\n    pub stalled_certainty: Certainty,\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":109,"oldLines":10,"newStart":109,"newLines":10,"lines":["     fn compute_coerce_goal(&mut self, goal: Goal<I, ty::CoercePredicate<I>>) -> QueryResult<I> {","         self.compute_subtype_goal(Goal {","             param_env: goal.param_env,","-            predicate: ty::SubtypePredicate {","+            predicate: ty::SubtypeRelation {","                 a_is_expected: false,","-                a: goal.predicate.a,","-                b: goal.predicate.b,","+                sub_ty: goal.predicate.a,","+                super_ty: goal.predicate.b,","             },","         })","     }"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

The next-generation trait solver, currently still WIP.\n//!\n//! As a user of rust, you can use `-Znext-solver` to enable the new trait solver.\n//!\n//! As a developer of rustc, you shouldn't be using the new trait\n//! solver without asking the trait-system-refactor-initiative, but it can\n//! be enabled with `InferCtxtBuilder::with_next_trait_solver`. This will\n//! ensure that trait solving using that inference context will be routed\n//! to the new trait solver.\n//!\n//! We now instead used a constant\n/// here. self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self))]\n    fn compute_region_outlives_goal(\n        &mut self,\n              // FIXME(generic_const_exprs): Implement handling for generic\n                // const expressions here.\n                if let Some(_normalized) = self.evaluate_const(param_env, uv) self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS)\n                }\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Infer(_) => {\n                self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS)\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Placeholder(_) | ty::ConstKind::Value(_) | ty::ConstKind::Error(_) => {\n                self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n            }\n            // We can freely ICE here as:\n            // - `Param` gets replaced with a placeholder during canonicalization\n            // - `Bound` cannot exist as we don't have a binder around the self Type\n            // - `Expr` is part of `feature(generic_const_exprs)` and is not implemented yet\n            ty::ConstKind::Param(_) | ty::ConstKind::Bound(_, _) | ty::ConstKind::Expr(_) => {\n                panic!(\"unexpected const kind: {:?}\", ct)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self), ret)]\n    fn compute_const_arg_has_type_goal(\n        &mut self,\n        goal: Goal<I, (I::Const, I::Ty)>,\n    ) -> QueryResult<I> {\n        let (ct, self.cx().type_of(uv.def.into()).instantiate(self.cx(), uv.args)\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Expr(_) => unimplemented!(\n                \"`feature(generic_const_exprs)` is not self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug)]\nenum MergeCandidateInfo {\n    AlwaysApplicable(usize),\n    EqualResponse,\n}\n\nimpl<D, I> EvalCtxt<'_, D>\nwhere\n    D: SolverDelegate<Interner = I>,\n    I: Interner,\n{\n    /// Try to merge multiple possible ways to prove a goal, if that is not possible returns `None`.\n    ///\n    /// In this case we tend to flounder and return ambiguity by calling `[EvalCtxt::flounder]`.\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self), ret)]\n    fn try_merge_candidates(\n        &mut self,\n        candidates: &[Candidate<I>],\n    ) -> Option<(CanonicalResponse<I>, MergeCandidateInfo)> {\n        if candidates.is_empty() {\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        let always_applicable = candidates.iter().enumerate().find(|(_, candidate)| {\n            candidate.result.value.certainty == Certainty::Yes\n                && has_no_inference_or_external_constraints(candidate.result)\n        });\n        if let Some((i, c)) = always_applicable {\n            return Some((c.result, MergeCandidateInfo::AlwaysApplicable(i)));\n        }\n\n        let one: CanonicalResponse<I> = candidates[0].result;\n        if candidates[1..].iter().all(|candidate| candidate.result == one) {\n            return Some((one, MergeCandidateInfo::EqualResponse));\n        }\n\n        None\n    }\n\n    fn bail_with_ambiguity(&mut self, candidates: &[Candidate<I>]) -> CanonicalResponse<I> {\n        debug_assert!(candidates.len() > 1);\n        let (cause, opaque_types_jank) = candidates.iter().fold(\n            (MaybeCause::Ambiguity, OpaqueTypesJank::AllGood),\n            |(c, jank), candidates| {\n                // We pull down the certainty of `Certainty::Yes` to ambiguity when combining\n                // these responses, b/c we're combining more than one response and this we\n                // don't know which one applies.\n                match candidates.result.value.certainty {\n                    Certainty::Yes => (c, jank),\n                    Certainty::Maybe { cause, opaque_types_jank } => {\n                        (c.or(cause), jank.or(opaque_types_jank))\n                    }\n                }\n            },\n        );\n        self.make_ambiguous_response_no_constraints(cause, opaque_types_jank)\n    }\n\n    /// If we fail to merge responses we flounder and return overflow or ambiguity.\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self), ret)]\n    fn flounder(&mut self, candidates: &[Candidate<I>]) -> QueryResult<I> {\n        if candidates.is_empty() {\n            return Err(NoSolution);\n        } else {\n            Ok(self.bail_with_ambiguity(candidates))\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Normalize a type for when it is structurally matched on.\n    ///\n    /// This function is necessary in nearly all cases before matching on a type.\n    /// Not doing so is likely to be incomplete and therefore unsound during\n    /// coherence.\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self, param_env), ret)]\n    fn structurally_normalize_ty(\n        &mut self,\n        param_env: I::ParamEnv,\n        ty: I::Ty,\n    ) -> Result<I::Ty, NoSolution> {\n        self.structurally_normalize_term(param_env, ty.into()).map(|term| term.expect_ty())\n    }\n\n    /// Normalize a const for when it is structurally matched on, or more likely\n    /// when it needs `.try_to_*` called on it (e.g. to turn it into Result<I::Const, NoSolution> {\n        self.structurally_normalize_term(param_env, ct.into()).map(|term| term.expect_const())\n    }\n\n    /// Normalize a term for when it is structurally matched on.\n    ///\n    /// This function is necessary in nearly all cases before matching on a ty/const.\n    /// Not doing so is likely to be incomplete and therefore unsound during coherence.\n    fn structurally_normalize_term(\n        &mut self,\n        param_env: I::ParamEnv,\n        term: I::Term,\n    ) -> Result<I::Term, NoSolution> {\n        if let Some(_) = term.to_alias_term() {\n ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                    term,\n                    normalized_term,\n                    ty::AliasRelationDirection::Equate,\n                ),\n            );\n self.try_evaluate_added_goals()?;\n            Ok(self.resolve_vars_if_possible(normalized_term))\n        } else {\n            Ok(term)\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn opaque_type_is_rigid(&self, def_id: I::DefId) -> bool {\n        match self.typing_mode() {\n            // Opaques are never rigid outside of analysis mode.\n            TypingMode::Coherence | TypingMode::PostAnalysis => false,\n            // During analysis, opaques are rigid unless they may be defined by\n            // the current body.\n            TypingMode::Analysis { defining_opaque_types_and_generators: non_rigid_opaques }\n            | TypingMode::Borrowck { defining_opaque_types: non_rigid_opaques }\n            | TypingMode::PostBorrowckAnalysis { defined_opaque_types: non_rigid_opaques } => {\n                !def_id.as_local().is_some_and(|def_id| non_rigid_opaques.contains(&def_id))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// The result of evaluating a goal.\n#[derive_where(Debug; I: Interner)]\npub struct GoalEvaluation<I: Interner> {\n    /// The goal we've evaluated. This is the input goal, but potentially with its\n    /// inference variables resolved. This never applies any inference constraints\n    /// from evaluating the goal.\n    ///\n    /// We rely on this to check whether root goals in HIR typeck had an unresolved\n    /// type inference variable in the input. We must not resolve this after evaluating\n    /// the goal as even if the inference variable has been resolved by evaluating the\n    /// goal itself, this goal may still end up failing due to region uniquification\n    /// later on.\n    ///\n    /// This is used as a minor optimization to avoid re-resolving inference variables\n    /// when reevaluating ambiguous goals. E.g. if we've got a goal `?x: Trait` with `?x`\n    /// already being constrained to `Vec<?y>`, then the first evaluation resolves it to\n    /// `Vec<?y>: Trait`. If this goal is still ambiguous and we later resolve `?y` to `u32`,\n    /// then reevaluating this goal now only needs to resolve `?y` while it would otherwise\n    /// have to resolve both `?x` and `?y`,\n    pub goal: Goal<I, I::Predicate>,\n    pub certainty: Certainty,\n    pub has_changed: HasChanged,\n    /// If the [`Certainty`] was `Maybe`, then keep track of whether the goal has changed\n    /// before rerunning it.\n    pub stalled_on: Option<GoalStalledOn<I>>,\n}\n\n/// The conditions that must change for a goal to warrant\n#[derive_where(Clone, Debug;                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.obligations.push(Obligation::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n rustc_middle::traits::solve::Goal;\nuse rustc_middle::ty::relate::combine::{super_combine_consts, super_combine_tys};\nuse rustc_middle::ty::relate::{\n    Relate, RelateResult, TypeRelation, relate_args_invariantly, relate_args_with_variances,\n};\nuse rustc_middle::ty::{self, DelayedSet, Ty, TyCtxt, TyVar};\nuse rustc_span::Span;\nuse tracing::{debug, instrument};\n\nuse crate::infer::BoundRegionConversionTime::HigherRankedType;\nuse crate::infer::relate::{PredicateEmittingRelation, StructurallyRelateAliases};\nuse crate::infer::{DefineOpaqueTypes, InferCtxt, SubregionOrigin, TypeTrace};\nuse crate::traits::{Obligation, PredicateObligations};\n\n/// Enforce that `a` is equal to or a subtype of `b`.\npub(crate) struct TypeRelating<'infcx, 'tcx> {\n    infcx: &'infcx InferCtxt<'tcx>,\n\n    // Immutable fields\n    trace: TypeTrace<'tcx>,\n    param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n    define_opaque_types: DefineOpaqueTypes,\n\n    // Mutable fields.\n    ambient_variance: ty::Variance,\n    obligations: PredicateObligations<'tcx>,\n    /// The cache only tracks the `ambient_variance` as it's the\n    /// only field which is mutable and which meaningfully changes\n    /// the result when relating types.\n    ///\n    /// The cache does not track whether the state of the\n    /// `InferCtxt` has been changed or whether we've added any\n    /// obligations to `self.goals`. Whether a goal is added\n    /// once or multiple times is not really meaningful.\n    ///\n    /// Changes in the inference state may delay some type inference to\n    /// the next fulfillment loop. Given that this loop is already\n    /// necessary, this is also not a meaningful change. Consider\n    /// the following three relations:\n    /// ```text\n    /// Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>\n    /// ?0 eq u32\n    /// Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>\n    /// ```\n    /// Without a cache, the second `Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>` would eagerly\n    /// constrain `?1` to `u32`. When using the cache entry from the\n    /// first time we've related these types, this only happens when\n    /// later proving the `Subtype(?0, ?1)` goal from the first relation.\n    cache: DelayedSet<(ty::Variance, Ty<'tcx>, Ty<'tcx>)>,\n}\n\nimpl<'infcx, 'tcx> TypeRelating<'infcx, 'tcx> {\n    pub(crate) fn new(\n        infcx: &'infcx InferCtxt<'tcx>,\n        trace: TypeTrace<'tcx>,\n        param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n        define_opaque_types: DefineOpaqueTypes,\n        ambient_variance: ty::Variance,\n    ) -> TypeRelating<'infcx, 'tcx> {\n        assert!(!infcx.next_trait_solver);\n        TypeRelating {\n            infcx,\n            trace,\n            param_env,\n            define_opaque_types,\n            ambient_variance,\n            obligations: PredicateObligations::new(),\n            cache: Default::default(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn into_obligations(self) -> PredicateObligations<'tcx> {\n        self.obligations\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> TypeRelation<TyCtxt<'tcx>> for TypeRelating<'_, 'tcx> {\n    fn cx(&self) -> TyCtxt<'tcx> {\n        self.infcx.tcx\n    }\n\n    fn relate_item_args(\n        &mut self,\n        item_def_id: rustc_hir::def_id::DefId,\n        a_arg: ty::GenericArgsRef<'tcx>,\n        b_arg: ty::GenericArgsRef<'tcx>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::GenericArgsRef<'tcx>> {\n        if self.ambient_variance == ty::Invariant {\n            // Avoid fetching the variance if we are in an invariant\n            // context; no need, and it can induce dependency cycles\n            // (e.g., #41849).\n            relate_args_invariantly(self, a_arg, b_arg)\n        } else {\n            let tcx = self.cx();\n            let opt_variances = tcx.variances_of(item_def_id);\n            relate_args_with_variances(self, item_def_id, opt_variances, a_arg, b_arg, false)\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn relate_with_variance<T: Relate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>>(\n        &mut self,\n        variance: ty::Variance,\n        _info: ty::VarianceDiagInfo<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n        a: T,\n        b: T,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, T> {\n        let old_ambient_variance = self.ambient_variance;\n        self.ambient_variance = self.ambient_variance.xform(variance);\n        debug!(?self.ambient_variance, \"new ambient variance\");\n\n        let r = if self.ambient_variance == ty::Bivariant { Ok(a) } else { self.relate(a, b) };\n\n        self.ambient_variance = old_ambient_variance;\n        r\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn tys(&mut self, a: Ty<'tcx>, b: Ty<'tcx>) -> RelateResult<'tcx, Ty<'tcx>> {\n        if a == b {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        let infcx = self.infcx;\n        let a = infcx.shallow_resolve(a);\n        let b = infcx.shallow_resolve(b);\n\n        if self.cache.contains(&(self.ambient_variance, a, b)) {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        match (a.kind(), b.kind()) {\n            (&ty::Infer(TyVar(a_id)), &ty::Infer(TyVar(b_id))) => {\n                match self.ambient_variance {\n                    ty::Covariant => {\n                        // can't make progress on `A <: B` if both A and B are\n                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.obligations.push(Obligation::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n                            self.trace.cause.clone(),\n                            self.param_env,\n                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypePredicate {\n                                a_is_expected: true,\n                                a,\n                                b,\n                            })),\n                        ));\n                    }\n                    ty::Contravariant => {\n                        // can't make progress on `B <: A` if both A and B are\n                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.obligations.push(Obligation::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n                            self.trace.cause.clone(),\n                            self.param_env,\n                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypePredicate {\n                                a_is_expected: false,\n                                a: b,\n                                b: a,\n                            })),\n                        ));\n                    }\n                    ty::Invariant => {\n                        infcx.inner.borrow_mut().type_variables().equate(a_id, b_id);\n                    }\n                    ty::Bivariant => {\n                        unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            (&ty::Infer(TyVar(a_vid)), _) => {\n                infcx.instantiate_ty_var(self, true, a_vid, self.ambient_variance, b)?;\n            }\n            (_, &ty::Infer(TyVar(b_vid))) => {\n                infcx.instantiate_ty_var(\n                    self,\n                    false,\n                    b_vid,\n                    self.ambient_variance.xform(ty::Contravariant),\n                    a,\n                )?;\n            }\n\n            (\n                &ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id: a_def_id, .. }),\n                &ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id: b_def_id, .. }),\n            ) if a_def_id == b_def_id => {\n                super_combine_tys(infcx, self, a, b)?;\n            }\n\n            (&ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id, .. }), _)\n            | (_, &ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id, .. }))\n                if self.define_opaque_types == DefineOpaqueTypes::Yes && def_id.is_local() =>\n            {\n                self.register_goals(infcx.handle_opaque_type(\n                    a,\n                    b,\n                    self.trace.cause.span,\n                    self.param_env(),\n                )?);\n            }\n\n            _ => {\n                super_combine_tys(infcx, self, a, b)?;\n            }\n        }\n\n        assert!(self.cache.insert((self.ambient_variance, a, b)));\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn regions(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Region<'tcx>,\n        b: ty::Region<'tcx>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::Region<'tcx>> {\n        let origin = SubregionOrigin::Subtype(Box::new(self.trace.clone()));\n\n        match self.ambient_variance {\n            // Subtype(&'a u8, &'b u8) => Outlives('a: 'b) => SubRegion('b, 'a)\n            ty::Covariant => {\n                self.infcx\n                    .inner\n                    .borrow_mut()\n                    .unwrap_region_constraints()\n                    .make_subregion(origin, b, a);\n            }\n            // Suptype(&'a u8, &'b u8) => Outlives('b: 'a) => SubRegion('a, 'b)\n            ty::Contravariant => {\n                self.infcx\n                    .inner\n                    .borrow_mut()\n                    .unwrap_region_constraints()\n                    .make_subregion(origin, a, b);\n            }\n            ty::Invariant => {\n                self.infcx\n                    .inner\n                    .borrow_mut()\n                    .unwrap_region_constraints()\n                    .make_eqregion(origin, a, b);\n            }\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn consts(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Const<'tcx>,\n        b: ty::Const<'tcx>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::Const<'tcx>> {\n        super_combine_consts(self.infcx, self, a, b)\n    }\n\n    fn binders<T>(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Binder<'tcx, T>,\n        b: ty::Binder<'tcx, T>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::Binder<'tcx, T>>\n    where\n        T: Relate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n    {\n        if a == b {\n            // Do nothing\n        } else if let Some(a) = a.no_bound_vars()\n            && let Some(b) = b.no_bound_vars()\n        {\n            self.relate(a, b)?;\n        } else {\n            let span = self.trace.cause.span;\n            let infcx = self.infcx;\n\n            match self.ambient_variance {\n                // Checks whether `for<..> sub <: for<..> sup` holds.\n                //\n                // For this to hold, **all** instantiations of the super type\n                // have to be a super type of **at least one** instantiation of\n                // the subtype.\n                //\n                // This is implemented by first entering a new universe.\n                // We then replace all bound variables in `sup` with placeholders,\n                // and all bound variables in `sub` with inference vars.\n                // We can then just relate the two resulting types as normal.\n                //\n                // Note: this is a subtle algorithm. For a full explanation, please see\n                // the [rustc dev guide][rd]\n                //\n                // [rd]: https://rustc-dev-guide.rust-lang.org/borrow_check/region_inference/placeholders_and_universes.html\n                ty::Covariant => {\n                    infcx.enter_forall(b, |b| {\n                        let a = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, a);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n                }\n                ty::Contravariant => {\n                    infcx.enter_forall(a, |a| {\n                        let b = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, b);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n                }\n\n                // When **equating** binders, we check that there is a 1-to-1\n                // correspondence between the bound vars in both types.\n                //\n                // We do so by separately instantiating one of the binders with\n                // placeholders and the other with inference variables and then\n                // equating the instantiated types.\n                //\n                // We want `for<..> A == for<..> B` -- therefore we want\n                // `exists<..> A == for<..> B` and `exists<..> B == for<..> A`.\n                // Check if `exists<..> A == for<..> B`\n                ty::Invariant => {\n                    infcx.enter_forall(b, |b| {\n                        let a = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, a);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n\n                    // Check if `exists<..> B == for<..> A`.\n                    infcx.enter_forall(a, |a| {\n                        let b = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, b);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n                }\n                ty::Bivariant => {\n                    unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> PredicateEmittingRelation<InferCtxt<'tcx>> for TypeRelating<'_, 'tcx> {\n    fn span(&self) -> Span {\n        self.trace.span()\n    }\n\n    fn param_env(&self) -> ty::ParamEnv<'tcx> {\n        self.param_env\n    }\n\n    fn structurally_relate_aliases(&self) -> StructurallyRelateAliases {\n        StructurallyRelateAliases::No\n    }\n\n    fn register_predicates(\n        &mut self,\n        preds: impl IntoIterator<Item: ty::Upcast<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Predicate<'tcx>>>,\n    ) {\n        self.obligations.extend(preds.into_iter().map(|pred| {\n            Obligation::new(self.infcx.tcx, self.trace.cause.clone(), self.param_env, pred)\n        }))\n    }\n\n    fn register_goals(&mut self, goals: impl IntoIterator<Item = Goal<'tcx, ty::Predicate<'tcx>>>) {\n        self.obligations.extend(goals.into_iter().map(|goal| {\n            Obligation::new(\n                self.infcx.tcx,\n                self.trace.cause.clone(),\n                goal.param_env,\n                goal.predicate,\n            )\n        }))\n    }\n\n    fn register_alias_relate_predicate(&mut self, a: Ty<'tcx>, b: Ty<'tcx>) {\n        self.register_predicates([ty::Binder::dummy(match self.ambient_variance {\n            ty::Covariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                a.into(),\n                b.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Subtype,\n            ),\n            // a :> b is b <: a\n            ty::Contravariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                b.into(),\n                a.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Subtype,\n            ),\n            ty::Invariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                a.into(),\n                b.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Equate,\n            ),\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        })]);\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":138,"oldLines":10,"newStart":138,"newLines":10,"lines":["                             self.cx(),","                             self.trace.cause.clone(),","                             self.param_env,","-                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypePredicate {","+ RelateResult, TypeRelation, relate_args_invariantly, relate_args_with_variances,\n};\nuse rustc_middle::ty::{self, DelayedSet, Ty, TyCtxt, TyVar};\nuse rustc_span::Span;\nuse tracing::{debug, instrument};\n\nuse crate::infer::BoundRegionConversionTime::HigherRankedType;\nuse crate::infer::relate::{PredicateEmittingRelation, StructurallyRelateAliases};\nuse crate::infer::{DefineOpaqueTypes, InferCtxt, SubregionOrigin, TypeTrace};\nuse crate::traits::{Obligation, PredicateObligations};\n\n/// Enforce that `a` is equal to or a subtype of `b`.\npub(crate) struct TypeRelating<'infcx, 'tcx> {\n    infcx: &'infcx InferCtxt<'tcx>,\n\n    // Immutable fields\n    trace: TypeTrace<'tcx>,\n    param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n    define_opaque_types: DefineOpaqueTypes,\n\n    // Mutable fields.\n    ambient_variance: ty::Variance,\n    obligations: PredicateObligations<'tcx>,\n    /// The cache only tracks the `ambient_variance` as it's the\n    /// only field which is mutable and which meaningfully changes\n    /// the result when relating types.\n    ///\n    /// The cache does not track whether the state of the\n    /// `InferCtxt` has been changed or whether we've added any\n    /// obligations to `self.goals`. Whether a goal is added\n    /// once or multiple times is not really meaningful.\n    ///\n    /// Changes in the inference state may delay some type inference to\n    /// the next fulfillment loop. Given that this loop is already\n    /// necessary, this is also not a meaningful change. Consider\n    /// the following three relations:\n    /// ```text\n    /// Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>\n    /// ?0 eq u32\n    /// Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>\n    /// ```\n    /// Without a cache, the second `Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>` would eagerly\n    /// constrain `?1` to `u32`. When using the cache entry from the\n    /// first time we've related these types, this only happens when\n    /// later proving the `Subtype(?0, ?1)` goal from the first relation.\n    cache: DelayedSet<(ty::Variance, Ty<'tcx>, Ty<'tcx>)>,\n}\n\nimpl<'infcx, 'tcx> TypeRelating<'infcx, 'tcx> {\n    pub(crate) fn new(\n        infcx: &'infcx InferCtxt<'tcx>,\n        trace: TypeTrace<'tcx>,\n        param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n        define_opaque_types: DefineOpaqueTypes,\n        ambient_variance: ty::Variance,\n    ) -> TypeRelating<'infcx, 'tcx> {\n        assert!(!infcx.next_trait_solver);\n        TypeRelating {\n            infcx,\n            trace,\n            param_env,\n            define_opaque_types,\n            ambient_variance,\n            obligations: PredicateObligations::new(),\n            cache: Default::default(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn into_obligations(self) -> PredicateObligations<'tcx> {\n        self.obligations\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> TypeRelation<TyCtxt<'tcx>> for TypeRelating<'_, 'tcx> {\n    fn cx(&self) -> TyCtxt<'tcx> {\n        self.infcx.tcx\n    }\n\n    fn relate_item_args(\n        &mut self,\n        item_def_id: rustc_hir::def_id::DefId,\n        a_arg: ty::GenericArgsRef<'tcx>,\n        b_arg: ty::GenericArgsRef<'tcx>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::GenericArgsRef<'tcx>> {\n        if self.ambient_variance == ty::Invariant {\n            // Avoid fetching the variance if we are in an invariant\n            // context; no need, and it can induce dependency cycles\n            // (e.g., #41849).\n            relate_args_invariantly(self, a_arg, b_arg)\n        } else {\n            let tcx = self.cx();\n            let opt_variances = tcx.variances_of(item_def_id);\n            relate_args_with_variances(self, item_def_id, opt_variances, a_arg, b_arg, false)\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn relate_with_variance<T: Relate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>>(\n        &mut self,\n        variance: ty::Variance,\n        _info: ty::VarianceDiagInfo<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n        a: T,\n        b: T,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, T> {\n        let old_ambient_variance = self.ambient_variance;\n        self.ambient_variance = self.ambient_variance.xform(variance);\n        debug!(?self.ambient_variance, \"new ambient variance\");\n\n        let r = if self.ambient_variance == ty::Bivariant { Ok(a) } else { self.relate(a, b) };\n\n        self.ambient_variance = old_ambient_variance;\n        r\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn tys(&mut self, a: Ty<'tcx>, b: Ty<'tcx>) -> RelateResult<'tcx, Ty<'tcx>> {\n        if a == b {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        let infcx = self.infcx;\n        let a = infcx.shallow_resolve(a);\n        let b = infcx.shallow_resolve(b);\n\n        if self.cache.contains(&(self.ambient_variance, a, b)) {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        match (a.kind(), b.kind()) {\n            (&ty::Infer(TyVar(a_id)), &ty::Infer(TyVar(b_id))) => {\n                match self.ambient_variance {\n                    ty::Covariant => {\n                        // can't make progress on `A <: B` if both A and B are\n                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.obligations.push(Obligation::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n                            self.trace.cause.clone(),\n                            self.param_env,\n                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation {\n                                a_is_expected: true,\n                                sub_ty: a,\n                                super_ty: b,\n                            })),\n                        ));\n                    }\n                    ty::Contravariant => {\n                        // can't make progress on `B <: A` if both A and B are\n                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.obligations.push(Obligation::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n                            self.trace.cause.clone(),\n a: b,\n                                b: a,\n                            })),\n                        ));\n                    }\n                    ty::Invariant => {\n                        infcx.inner.borrow_mut().type_variables().equate(a_id, b_id);\n                    }\n                    ty::Bivariant => {\n                        unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            (&ty::Infer(TyVar(a_vid)), _) => {\n                infcx.instantiate_ty_var(self, true, a_vid, self.ambient_variance, b)?;\n            }\n            (_, &ty::Infer(TyVar(b_vid))) => {\n                infcx.instantiate_ty_var(\n                    self,\n                    false,\n                    b_vid,\n                    self.ambient_variance.xform(ty::Contravariant),\n                    a,\n                )?;\n            }\n\n            (\n              if self.define_opaque_types == DefineOpaqueTypes::Yes && def_id.is_local() =>\n            {\n                self.register_goals(infcx.handle_opaque_type(\n                    a,\n                    b,\n                    self.trace.cause.span,\n                    self.param_env(),\n                )?);\n            }\n\n            _ => {\n                super_combine_tys(infcx, self, a, b)?;\n            }\n        }\n\n        assert!(self.cache.insert((self.ambient_variance, a, b)));\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn regions(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Region<'tcx>,\n        b: ty::Region<'tcx>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::Region<'tcx>> {\n        let origin = SubregionOrigin::Subtype(Box::new(self.trace.clone()));\n\n        match self.ambient_variance {\n            // Subtype(&'a u8, &'b u8) => Outlives('a: 'b) => SubRegion('b, 'a)\n            ty::Covariant => {\n                self.infcx\n                    .inner\n                    .borrow_mut()\n                    .unwrap_region_constraints()\n                    .make_subregion(origin, b, a);\n            }\n            // Suptype(&'a u8, &'b u8) => Outlives('b: 'a) => SubRegion('a, 'b)\n            ty::Contravariant => {\n                self.infcx\n                    .inner\n                    .borrow_mut()\n                    .unwrap_region_constraints()\n                    .make_subregion(origin, a, b);\n            }\n            ty::Invariant => {\n                self.infcx\n                    .inner\n                    .borrow_mut()\n                    .unwrap_region_constraints()\n                    .make_eqregion(origin, a, b);\n            }\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn consts(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Const<'tcx>,\n        b: ty::Const<'tcx>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::Const<'tcx>> {\n        super_combine_consts(self.infcx, self, a, b)\n    }\n\n    fn binders<T>(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Binder<'tcx, T>,\n        b: ty::Binder<'tcx, T>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::Binder<'tcx, T>>\n    where\n        T: Relate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n    {\n        if a == b {\n            // Do nothing\n        } else if let Some(a) = a.no_bound_vars()\n            && let Some(b) = b.no_bound_vars()\n        {\n            self.relate(a, b)?;\n        } else {\n            let span = self.trace.cause.span;\n            let infcx = self.infcx;\n\n            match self.ambient_variance {\n                // Checks whether `for<..> sub <: for<..> sup` holds.\n                //\n                // For this to hold, **all** instantiations of the super type\n                // have to be a super type of **at least one** instantiation of\n                // the subtype.\n                //\n                // This is implemented by first entering a new universe.\n                // We then replace all bound variables in `sup` with placeholders,\n                // and all bound variables in `sub` with inference vars.\n                // We can then just relate the two resulting types as normal.\n                //\n                // Note: this is a subtle algorithm. For a full explanation, please see\n                // the [rustc dev guide][rd]\n                //\n                // [rd]: https://rustc-dev-guide.rust-lang.org/borrow_check/region_inference/placeholders_and_universes.html\n                ty::Covariant => {\n                    infcx.enter_forall(b, |b| {\n                        let a = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, a);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n                }\n                ty::Contravariant => {\n                    infcx.enter_forall(a, |a| {\n                        let b = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, b);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n                }\n\n                // When **equating** binders, we check that there is a 1-to-1\n                // correspondence between the bound vars in both types.\n                //\n                // We do so by separately instantiating one of the binders with\n                // placeholders and the other with inference variables and then\n                // equating the instantiated types.\n                //\n                // We want `for<..> A == for<..> B` -- therefore we want\n                // `exists<..> A == for<..> B` and `exists<..> B == for<..> A`.\n                // Check if `exists<..> A == for<..> B`\n                ty::Invariant => {\n                    infcx.enter_forall(b, |b| {\n                        let a = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, a);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n\n                    // Check if `exists<..> B == for<..> A`.\n                    infcx.enter_forall(a, |a| {\n                        let b = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, b);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n                }\n                ty::Bivariant => {\n                    unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> PredicateEmittingRelation<InferCtxt<'tcx>> for TypeRelating<'_, 'tcx> {\n    fn span(&self) -> Span {\n        self.trace.span()\n    }\n\n    fn param_env(&self) -> ty::ParamEnv<'tcx> {\n        self.param_env\n    }\n\n    fn structurally_relate_aliases(&self) -> StructurallyRelateAliases {\n        StructurallyRelateAliases::No\n    }\n\n    fn register_predicates(\n        &mut self,\n        preds: impl IntoIterator<Item: ty::Upcast<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Predicate<'tcx>>>,\n    ) {\n        self.obligations.extend(preds.into_iter().map(|pred| {\n            Obligation::new(self.infcx.tcx, self.trace.cause.clone(), self.param_env, pred)\n        }))\n    }\n\n    fn register_goals(&mut self, goals: impl IntoIterator<Item = Goal<'tcx, ty::Predicate<'tcx>>>) {\n        self.obligations.extend(goals.into_iter().map(|goal| {\n            Obligation::new(\n                self.infcx.tcx,\n                self.trace.cause.clone(),\n                goal.param_env,\n                goal.predicate,\n            )\n        }))\n    }\n\n    fn register_alias_relate_predicate(&mut self, a: Ty<'tcx>, b: Ty<'tcx>) {\n        self.register_predicates([ty::Binder::dummy(match self.ambient_variance {\n            ty::Covariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                a.into(),\n                b.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Subtype,\n            ),\n            // a :> b is b <: a\n            ty::Contravariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                b.into(),\n                a.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Subtype,\n            ),\n            ty::Invariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                a.into(),\n                b.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Equate,\n            ),\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        })]);\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":152,"oldLines":10,"newStart":152,"newLines":10,"lines":["                             self.cx(),","                             self.trace.cause.clone(),"," This is partly a micro-optimization, but it's\n        // also an opportunity to \"sub-unify\" the variables. This isn't\n        // *necessary* to prevent cycles, because they would eventually be sub-unified\n        // anyhow during generalization, but it helps with diagnostics (we can detect\n        // earlier that they are sub-unified).\n        //\n        // Note that we can just skip the binders here because\n        // type variables can't (at present, at\n        // least) capture any of the things bound by this binder.\n        //\n        // Note that this sub here is not just for diagnostics - it has semantic\n        // effects as well.\n        let r_a = self.shallow_resolve(predicate.skip_binder().a);\n        let r_b = self.shallow_resolve(predicate.skip_binder().b);\n        match (r_a.kind(), r_b.kind()) {\n            (&ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(a_vid)), &ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(b_vid))) => {\n                self.sub_unify_ty_vids_raw(a_vid, b_vid);\n                return Err((a_vid, b_vid));\n            }\n            _ => {}\n        }\n\n        self.enter_forall(predicate, |ty::SubtypePredicate { a_is_expected, a, b }| {\n            if a_is_expected {\n                Ok(self.at(cause, param_env).sub(DefineOpaqueTypes::Yes, a, b))\n            } else {\n                Ok(self.at(cause, param_env).sup(DefineOpaqueTypes::Yes, b, a))\n            }\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Number of type variables created so far.\n    pub fn num_ty_vars(&self) -> usize {\n        self.inner.borrow_mut().type_variables().num_vars()\n    }\n\n    pub fn next_ty_vid(&self, span: Span) -> TyVid {\n        self.next_ty_vid_with_origin(TypeVariableOrigin { span, param_def_id: None })","numLines":60,"startLine":712,"totalLines":1697}}}
