std::ops::ControlFlow;\n\nuse rustc_data_structures::fx::FxHashSet;\nuse rustc_data_structures::graph::vec_graph::VecGraph;\nuse rustc_data_structures::graph::{self};\nuse rustc_data_structures::unord::{UnordMap, UnordSet};\nuse rustc_hir as hir;\nuse rustc_hir::HirId;\nuse rustc_hir::def::{DefKind, Res};\nuse rustc_hir::def_id::DefId;\nuse rustc_hir::intravisit::{InferKind, Visitor};\nuse rustc_middle::ty::{self, Ty, TyCtxt, TypeSuperVisitable, TypeVisitable};\nuse rustc_session::lint;\nuse rustc_span::def_id::LocalDefId;\nuse rustc_span::{DUMMY_SP, Span};\nuse rustc_trait_selection::traits::{ObligationCause, ObligationCtxt};\nuse tracing::debug;\n\nuse crate::{FnCtxt, errors};\n\n#[derive(Copy, Clone)]\npub(crate) enum DivergingFallbackBehavior {\n    /// Always fallback to `()` (aka \"always spontaneous decay\")\n    ToUnit,\n    /// Always fallback to `!` (which should be equivalent to never falling back + not making\n    /// never-to-any coercions unless necessary)\n    ToNever,\n    /// Don't fallback at all\n    NoFallback,\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> FnCtxt<'_, 'tcx> {\n    /// Performs type inference fallback, setting [`FnCtxt::diverging_fallback_has_occurred`]\n    /// if the never type fallback has occurred.\n    pub(super) fn type_inference_fallback(&self) {\n        debug!(\n            \"type-inference-fallback start obligations: {:#?}\",\n            self.fulfillment_cx.borrow_mut().pending_obligations()\n        );\n\n        // All type checking constraints were added, try to fallback unsolved variables.\n        self.select_obligations_where_possible(|_| {});\n\n        debug!(\n            \"type-inference-fallback post selection obligations: {:#?}\",\n            self.fulfillment_cx.borrow_mut().pending_obligations()\n        );\n\n        let fallback_occurred = self.fallback_types();\n\n        if fallback_occurred {\n            // if fallback occurred, previously stalled goals may make progress again\n            self.select_obligations_where_possible(|_| {});\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn fallback_types(&self) -> bool {\n        // Check if we have any unresolved variables. If not, no need for fallback.\n        let unresolved_variables = self.unresolved_variables();\n\n        if unresolved_variables.is_empty() {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        let (diverging_fallback, diverging_fallback_ty) =\n            self.calculate_diverging_fallback(&unresolved_variables);\n\n        // We do fallback in two passes, to try to generate\n        // better error messages.\n        // The first time, we do *not* replace opaque types.\n        let mut fallback_occurred = false;\n        for ty in unresolved_variables {\n            debug!(\"unsolved_variable = {:?}\", ty);\n            fallback_occurred |=\n                self.fallback_if_possible(ty, &diverging_fallback, diverging_fallback_ty);\n        }\n\n        fallback_occurred\n    }\n\n    /// Tries to apply a fallback to `ty` if it is an unsolved variable.\n    ///\n    /// - Unconstrained ints are replaced with `i32`.\n    ///\n    /// - Unconstrained floats are replaced with `f64`.\n    ///\n    /// - Non-numerics may get replaced with `()` or `!`, depending on how they\n    ///   were categorized by [`Self::calculate_diverging_fallback`], crate's\n    ///   edition, and the setting of `#![rustc_never_type_options(fallback = ...)]`.\n    ///\n    /// Fallback becomes very dubious if we have encountered\n    /// type-checking errors. In that case, fallback to Error.\n    ///\n    /// Sets [`FnCtxt::diverging_fallback_has_occurred`] if never type fallback\n    /// is performed during this call.\n    fn fallback_if_possible(\n        &self,\n        ty: Ty<'tcx>,\n        diverging_fallback: &UnordSet<Ty<'tcx>>,\n        diverging_fallback_ty: Ty<'tcx>,\n    ) -> bool {\n        // Careful: we do NOT shallow-resolve `ty`. We know that `ty`\n        // is an unsolved variable, and we determine its fallback\n        // based solely on how it was created, not what other type\n        // variables it may have been unified with since then.\n        //\n        // The reason this matters is that other attempts at fallback\n        // may (in principle) conflict with this fallback, and we wish\n        // to generate a type error in that case. (However, this\n        // actually isn't true right now, because we're only using the\n        // builtin fallback rules. This would be true if we were using\n        // user-supplied fallbacks. But it's still useful to write the\n        // code to detect bugs.)\n        //\n        // (Note though that if we have a general type variable `?T`\n        // that is then unified with an integer type variable `?I`\n        // that ultimately never gets resolved to a special integral\n        // type, `?T` is not considered unsolved, but `?I` is. The\n        // same is true for float variables.)\n        let fallback = match ty.kind() {\n            _ if let Some(e) = self.tainted_by_errors() => Ty::new_error(self.tcx, e),\n            ty::Infer(ty::IntVar(_)) => self.tcx.types.i32,\n            ty::Infer(ty::FloatVar(_)) => self.tcx.types.f64,\n            _ if diverging_fallback.contains(&ty) => {\n                self.diverging_fallback_has_occurred.set(true);\n                diverging_fallback_ty\n            }\n            _ => return false,\n        };\n        debug!(\"fallback_if_possible(ty={:?}): defaulting to `{:?}`\", ty, fallback);\n\n        let span = ty.ty_vid().map_or(DUMMY_SP, |vid| self.infcx.type_var_origin(vid).span);\n        self.demand_eqtype(span, ty, fallback);\n        true\n    }\n\n    fn calculate_diverging_fallback(\n        &self,\n        unresolved_variables: &[Ty<'tcx>],\n    ) -> (UnordSet<Ty<'tcx>>, Ty<'tcx>) {\n        debug!(\"calculate_diverging_fallback({:?})\", unresolved_variables);\n\n        let diverging_fallback_ty = match self.diverging_fallback_behavior {\n            DivergingFallbackBehavior::ToUnit => self.tcx.types.unit,\n            DivergingFallbackBehavior::ToNever => self.tcx.types.never,\n            DivergingFallbackBehavior::NoFallback => {\n                // the type doesn't matter, since no fallback will occur\n                return (UnordSet::new(), self.tcx.types.unit);\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Construct a coercion graph where an edge `A -> B` indicates\n        // a type variable is that is coerced\n        let coercion_graph = self.create_coercion_graph();\n\n        // Extract the unsolved type inference variable vids; note that some\n        // unsolved variables are integer/float variables and are excluded.\n        let unsolved_vids = unresolved_variables.iter().filter_map(|ty| ty.ty_vid());\n\n        // Compute the diverging root vids D -- that is, the root vid of\n        // those type variables that (a) are the target of a coercion from\n        // a `!` type and (b) have not yet been solved.\n        //\n        // These variables are the ones that are targets for fallback to\n        // either `!` or `()`.\n        let diverging_roots: UnordSet<ty::TyVid> = self\n            .diverging_type_vars\n            .borrow()\n            .items()\n            .map(|&ty| self.shallow_resolve(ty))\n            .filter_map(|ty| ty.ty_vid())\n            .map(|vid| self.root_var(vid))\n            .collect();\n        debug!(\n            \"calculate_diverging_fallback: diverging_type_vars={:?}\",\n            self.diverging_type_vars.borrow()\n        );\n        debug!(\"calculate_diverging_fallback: diverging_roots={:?}\", diverging_roots);\n\n        // Find all type variables that are reachable from a diverging\n        // type variable. These will typically default to `!`, unless\n        // we find later that they are *also* reachable from some\n        // other type variable outside this set.\n        let mut diverging_vids = vec![];\n        for unsolved_vid in unsolved_vids {\n            let root_vid = self.root_var(unsolved_vid);\n            debug!(\n                \"calculate_diverging_fallback: unsolved_vid={:?} root_vid={:?} diverges={:?}\",\n                unsolved_vid,\n                root_vid,\n                diverging_roots.contains(&root_vid),\n            );\n            if diverging_roots.contains(&root_vid) {\n                diverging_vids.push(unsolved_vid);\n\n                debug!(\n                    \"calculate_diverging_fallback: root_vid={:?} reaches {:?}\",\n                    root_vid,\n                    graph::depth_first_search(&coercion_graph, root_vid).collect::<Vec<_>>()\n                );\n            }\n        }\n\n        debug!(\"obligations: {:#?}\", self.fulfillment_cx.borrow_mut().pending_obligations());\n\n        let mut diverging_fallback = UnordSet::with_capacity(diverging_vids.len());\n        let unsafe_infer_vars = OnceCell::new();\n\n        self.lint_obligations_broken_by_never_type_fallback_change(\n            &diverging_vids,\n            &coercion_graph,\n        );\n\n        for &diverging_vid in &diverging_vids {\n            let diverging_ty = Ty::new_var(self.tcx, diverging_vid);\n            let root_vid = self.root_var(diverging_vid);\n\n            self.lint_never_type_fallback_flowing_into_unsafe_code(\n                &unsafe_infer_vars,\n                &coercion_graph,\n                root_vid,\n            );\n\n            diverging_fallback.insert(diverging_ty);\n        }\n\n        (diverging_fallback, diverging_fallback_ty)\n    }\n\n    fn lint_never_type_fallback_flowing_into_unsafe_code(\n        &self,\n        unsafe_infer_vars: &OnceCell<UnordMap<ty::TyVid, (HirId, Span, UnsafeUseReason)>>,\n        coercion_graph: &VecGraph<ty::TyVid, true>,\n        root_vid: ty::TyVid,\n    ) {\n        let unsafe_infer_vars = unsafe_infer_vars.get_or_init(|| {\n            let unsafe_infer_vars = compute_unsafe_infer_vars(self, self.body_id);\n            debug!(?unsafe_infer_vars);\n            unsafe_infer_vars\n        });\n\n        let affected_unsafe_infer_vars =\n            graph::depth_first_search_as_undirected(&coercion_graph, root_vid)\n                .filter_map(|x| unsafe_infer_vars.get(&x).copied())\n                .collect::<Vec<_>>();\n\n        let sugg = self.try_to_suggest_annotations(&[root_vid], coercion_graph);\n\n        for (hir_id, span, reason) in affected_unsafe_infer_vars {\n            self.tcx.emit_node_span_lint(\n                lint::builtin::NEVER_TYPE_FALLBACK_FLOWING_INTO_UNSAFE,\n                hir_id,\n                span,\n                match reason {\n                    UnsafeUseReason::Call => {\n                        errors::NeverTypeFallbackFlowingIntoUnsafe::Call { sugg: sugg.clone() }\n                    }\n                    UnsafeUseReason::Method => {\n                        errors::NeverTypeFallbackFlowingIntoUnsafe::Method { sugg: sugg.clone() }\n                    }\n                    UnsafeUseReason::Path => {\n                        errors::NeverTypeFallbackFlowingIntoUnsafe::Path { sugg: sugg.clone() }\n                    }\n                    UnsafeUseReason::UnionField => {\n                        errors::NeverTypeFallbackFlowingIntoUnsafe::UnionField {\n                            sugg: sugg.clone(),\n                        }\n                    }\n                    UnsafeUseReason::Deref => {\n                        errors::NeverTypeFallbackFlowingIntoUnsafe::Deref { sugg: sugg.clone() }\n                    }\n                },\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn lint_obligations_broken_by_never_type_fallback_change(\n        &self,\n        diverging_vids: &[ty::TyVid],\n        coercions: &VecGraph<ty::TyVid, true>,\n    ) {\n        let DivergingFallbackBehavior::ToUnit = self.diverging_fallback_behavior else { return };\n\n        // Fallback happens if and only if there are diverging variables\n        if diverging_vids.is_empty() {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Returns errors which happen if fallback is set to `fallback`\n        let remaining_errors_if_fallback_to = |fallback| {\n            self.probe(|_| {\n                let obligations = self.fulfillment_cx.borrow().pending_obligations();\n                let ocx = ObligationCtxt::new_with_diagnostics(&self.infcx);\n                ocx.register_obligations(obligations.iter().cloned());\n\n                for &diverging_vid in diverging_vids {\n                    let diverging_ty = Ty::new_var(self.tcx, diverging_vid);\n\n                    ocx.eq(&ObligationCause::dummy(), self.param_env, diverging_ty, fallback)\n                        .expect(\"expected diverging var to be unconstrained\");\n                }\n\n                ocx.try_evaluate_obligations()\n            })\n        };\n\n        // If we have no errors with `fallback = ()`, but *do* have errors with `fallback = !`,\n        // then this code will be broken by the never type fallback change.\n        let unit_errors = remaining_errors_if_fallback_to(self.tcx.types.unit);\n        if unit_errors.is_empty()\n            && let mut never_errors = remaining_errors_if_fallback_to(self.tcx.types.never)\n            && let [never_error, ..] = never_errors.as_mut_slice()\n        {\n            self.adjust_fulfillment_error_for_expr_obligation(never_error);\n            let sugg = self.try_to_suggest_annotations(diverging_vids, coercions);\n            self.tcx.emit_node_span_lint(\n                lint::builtin::DEPENDENCY_ON_UNIT_NEVER_TYPE_FALLBACK,\n                self.tcx.local_def_id_to_hir_id(self.body_id),\n                self.tcx.def_span(self.body_id),\n                errors::DependencyOnUnitNeverTypeFallback {\n                    obligation_span: never_error.obligation.cause.span,\n                    obligation: never_error.obligation.predicate,\n                    sugg,\n                },\n            )\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns a graph whose nodes are (unresolved) inference variables and where\n    /// an edge `?A -> ?B` indicates that the variable `?A` is coerced to `?B`.\n    fn create_coercion_graph(&self) -> VecGraph<ty::TyVid, true> {\n        let pending_obligations = self.fulfillment_cx.borrow_mut().pending_obligations();\n        debug!(\"create_coercion_graph: pending_obligations={:?}\", pending_obligations);\n        let coercion_edges: Vec<(ty::TyVid, ty::TyVid)> = pending_obligations\n            .into_iter()\n            .filter_map(|obligation| {\n                // The predicates we are looking for look like `Coerce(?A -> ?B)`.\n                // They will have no bound variables.\n                obligation.predicate.kind().no_bound_vars()\n            })\n            .filter_map(|atom| {\n                // We consider both subtyping and coercion to imply 'flow' from\n                // some position in the code `a` to a different position `b`.\n                // This is then used to determine which variables interact with\n                // live code, and as such must fall back to `()` to preserve\n                // soundness.\n                //\n                // In practice currently the two ways that this happens is\n                // coercion and subtyping.\n                let (a, b) = match atom {\n                    ty::PredicateKind::Coerce(ty::CoercePredicate { a, b }) => (a, b),\n                    ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypePredicate { a_is_expected: _, a, b }) => {\n                        (a, b)\n                    }\n                    _ => return None,\n                };\n\n                let a_vid = self.root_vid(a)?;\n                let b_vid = self.root_vid(b)?;\n                Some((a_vid, b_vid))\n            })\n            .collect();\n        debug!(\"create_coercion_graph: coercion_edges={:?}\", coercion_edges);\n        let num_ty_vars = self.num_ty_vars();\n\n        VecGraph::new(num_ty_vars, coercion_edges)\n    }\n\n    /// If `ty` is an unresolved type variable, returns its root vid.\n    fn root_vid(&self, ty: Ty<'tcx>) -> Option<ty::TyVid> {\n        Some(self.root_var(self.shallow_resolve(ty).ty_vid()?))\n    }\n\n    /// Given a set of diverging vids and coercions, walk the HIR to gather a\n    /// set of suggestions which can be applied to preserve fallback to unit.\n    fn try_to_suggest_annotations(\n        &self,\n        diverging_vids: &[ty::TyVid],\n        coercions: &VecGraph<ty::TyVid, true>,\n    ) -> errors::SuggestAnnotations {\n        let body =\n            self.tcx.hir_maybe_body_owned_by(self.body_id).expect(\"body id must have an owner\");\n        // For each diverging var, look through the HIR for a place to give it\n        // a type annotation. We do this per var because we only really need one\n        // suggestion to influence a var to be `()`.\n        let suggestions = diverging_vids\n            .iter()\n            .copied()\n            .filter_map(|vid| {\n                let reachable_vids =\n                    graph::depth_first_search_as_undirected(coercions, vid).collect();\n                AnnotateUnitFallbackVisitor { reachable_vids, fcx: self }\n                    .visit_expr(body.value)\n                    .break_value()\n            })\n            .collect();\n        errors::SuggestAnnotations { suggestions }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Try to walk the HIR to find a place to insert a useful suggestion\n/// to preserve fallback to `()` in 2024.\nstruct AnnotateUnitFallbackVisitor<'a, 'tcx> {\n    reachable_vids: FxHashSet<ty::TyVid>,\n    fcx: &'a FnCtxt<'a, 'tcx>,\n}\nimpl<'tcx> AnnotateUnitFallbackVisitor<'_, 'tcx> {\n    // For a given path segment, if it's missing a turbofish, try to suggest adding\n    // one so we can constrain an argument to `()`. To keep the suggestion simple,\n    // we want to simply suggest `_` for all the other args. This (for now) only\n    // works when there are only type variables (and region variables, since we can\n    // elide them)...\n    fn suggest_for_segment(\n        &self,\n        arg_segment: &'tcx hir::PathSegment<'tcx>,\n        def_id: DefId,\n        id: HirId,\n    ) -> ControlFlow<errors::SuggestAnnotation> {\n        if arg_segment.args.is_none()\n            && let Some(all_args) = self.fcx.typeck_results.borrow().node_args_opt(id)\n            && let generics = self.fcx.tcx.generics_of(def_id)\n            && let args = all_args[generics.parent_count..].iter().zip(&generics.own_params)\n            // We can't turbofish consts :(\n            && args.clone().all(|(_, param)| matches!(param.kind, ty::GenericParamDefKind::Type { .. } | ty::GenericParamDefKind::Lifetime))\n        {\n            // We filter out APITs, which are not turbofished.\n            let non_apit_type_args = args.filter(|(_, param)| {\n                matches!(param.kind, ty::GenericParamDefKind::Type { synthetic: false, .. })\n            });\n            let n_tys = non_apit_type_args.clone().count();\n            for (idx, (arg, _)) in non_apit_type_args.enumerate() {\n                if let Some(ty) = arg.as_type()\n                    && let Some(vid) = self.fcx.root_vid(ty)\n                    && self.reachable_vids.contains(&vid)\n                {\n                    return ControlFlow::Break(errors::SuggestAnnotation::Turbo(\n                        arg_segment.ident.span.shrink_to_hi(),\n                        n_tys,\n                        idx,\n                    ));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        ControlFlow::Continue(())\n    }\n}\nimpl<'tcx> Visitor<'tcx> for AnnotateUnitFallbackVisitor<'_, 'tcx> {\n    type Result = ControlFlow<errors::SuggestAnnotation>;\n\n    fn visit_infer(\n        &mut self,\n        inf_id: HirId,\n        inf_span: Span,\n        _kind: InferKind<'tcx>,\n    ) -> Self::Result {\n        // Try to replace `_` with `()`.\n        if let Some(ty) = self.fcx.typeck_results.borrow().node_type_opt(inf_id)\n            && let Some(vid) = self.fcx.root_vid(ty)\n            && self.reachable_vids.contains(&vid)\n            && inf_span.can_be_used_for_suggestions()\n        {\n            return ControlFlow::Break(errors::SuggestAnnotation::Unit(inf_span));\n        }\n\n        ControlFlow::Continue(())\n    }\n\n    fn visit_qpath(\n        &mut self,\n        qpath: &'tcx rustc_hir::QPath<'tcx>,\n        id: HirId,\n        span: Span,\n    ) -> Self::Result {\n        let arg_segment = match qpath {\n            hir::QPath::Resolved(_, path) => {\n                path.segments.last().expect(\"paths should have a segment\")\n            }\n            hir::QPath::TypeRelative(_, segment) => segment,\n        };\n        // Alternatively, try to turbofish `::<_, (), _>`.\n        if let Some(def_id) = self.fcx.typeck_results.borrow().qpath_res(qpath, id).opt_def_id()\n            && span.can_be_used_for_suggestions()\n        {\n            self.suggest_for_segment(arg_segment, def_id, id)?;\n        }\n        hir::intravisit::walk_qpath(self, qpath, id)\n    }\n\n    fn visit_expr(&mut self, expr: &'tcx hir::Expr<'tcx>) -> Self::Result {\n        if let hir::ExprKind::Closure(&hir::Closure { body, .. })\n        | hir::ExprKind::ConstBlock(hir::ConstBlock { body, .. }) = expr.kind\n        {\n            self.visit_body(self.fcx.tcx.hir_body(body))?;\n        }\n\n        // Try to suggest adding an explicit qself `()` to a trait method path.\n        // i.e. changing `Default::default()` to `<() as Default>::default()`.\n        if let hir::ExprKind::Path(hir::QPath::Resolved(None, path)) = expr.kind\n            && let Res::Def(DefKind::AssocFn, def_id) = path.res\n            && self.fcx.tcx.trait_of_assoc(def_id).is_some()\n            && let Some(args) = self.fcx.typeck_results.borrow().node_args_opt(expr.hir_id)\n            && let self_ty = args.type_at(0)\n            && let Some(vid) = self.fcx.root_vid(self_ty)\n            && self.reachable_vids.contains(&vid)\n            && let [.., trait_segment, _method_segment] = path.segments\n            && expr.span.can_be_used_for_suggestions()\n        {\n            let span = path.span.shrink_to_lo().to(trait_segment.ident.span);\n            return ControlFlow::Break(errors::SuggestAnnotation::Path(span));\n        }\n\n        // Or else, try suggesting turbofishing the method args.\n        if let hir::ExprKind::MethodCall(segment, ..) = expr.kind\n            && let Some(def_id) =\n                self.fcx.typeck_results.borrow().type_dependent_def_id(expr.hir_id)\n            && expr.span.can_be_used_for_suggestions()\n        {\n            self.suggest_for_segment(segment, def_id, expr.hir_id)?;\n        }\n\n        hir::intravisit::walk_expr(self, expr)\n    }\n\n    fn visit_local(&mut self, local: &'tcx hir::LetStmt<'tcx>) -> Self::Result {\n        // For a local, try suggest annotating the type if it's missing.\n        if let hir::LocalSource::Normal = local.source\n            && let None = local.ty\n            && let Some(ty) = self.fcx.typeck_results.borrow().node_type_opt(local.hir_id)\n            && let Some(vid) = self.fcx.root_vid(ty)\n            && self.reachable_vids.contains(&vid)\n            && local.span.can_be_used_for_suggestions()\n        {\n            return ControlFlow::Break(errors::SuggestAnnotation::Local(\n                local.pat.span.shrink_to_hi(),\n            ));\n        }\n        hir::intravisit::walk_local(self, local)\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Copy, Clone)]\npub(crate) enum UnsafeUseReason {\n    Call,\n    Method,\n    Path,\n    UnionField,\n    Deref,\n}\n\n/// Finds all type variables which are passed to an `unsafe` operation.\n///\n/// For example, for this function `f`:\n/// ```ignore (demonstrative)\n/// fn f() {\n///     unsafe {\n///         let x /* ?X */ = core::mem::zeroed();\n///         //               ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ -- hir_id, span, reason\n///\n///         let y = core::mem::zeroed::<Option<_ /* ?Y */>>();\n///         //      ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ -- hir_id, span, reason\n///     }\n/// }\n/// ```\n///\n/// `compute_unsafe_infer_vars` will return `{ id(?X) -> (hir_id, span, Call) }`\nfn compute_unsafe_infer_vars<'a, 'tcx>(\n    fcx: &'a FnCtxt<'a, 'tcx>,\n    body_id: LocalDefId,\n) -> UnordMap<ty::TyVid, (HirId, Span, UnsafeUseReason)> {\n    let body = fcx.tcx.hir_maybe_body_owned_by(body_id).expect(\"body id must have an owner\");\n    let mut res = UnordMap::default();\n\n    struct UnsafeInferVarsVisitor<'a, 'tcx> {\n        fcx: &'a FnCtxt<'a, 'tcx>,\n        res: &'a mut UnordMap<ty::TyVid, (HirId, Span, UnsafeUseReason)>,\n    }\n\n    impl Visitor<'_> for UnsafeInferVarsVisitor<'_, '_> {\n        fn visit_expr(&mut self, ex: &'_ hir::Expr<'_>) {\n            let typeck_results = self.fcx.typeck_results.borrow();\n\n            match ex.kind {\n                hir::ExprKind::MethodCall(..) => {\n                    if let Some(def_id) = typeck_results.type_dependent_def_id(ex.hir_id)\n                        && let method_ty = self.fcx.tcx.type_of(def_id).instantiate_identity()\n                        && let sig = method_ty.fn_sig(self.fcx.tcx)\n                        && sig.safety().is_unsafe()\n                    {\n                        let mut collector = InferVarCollector {\n                            value: (ex.hir_id, ex.span, UnsafeUseReason::Method),\n                            res: self.res,\n                        };\n\n                        // Collect generic arguments (incl. `Self`) of the method\n                        typeck_results\n                            .node_args(ex.hir_id)\n                            .types()\n                            .for_each(|t| t.visit_with(&mut collector));\n                    }\n                }\n\n                hir::ExprKind::Call(func, ..) => {\n                    let func_ty = typeck_results.expr_ty(func);\n\n                    if func_ty.is_fn()\n                        && let sig = func_ty.fn_sig(self.fcx.tcx)\n                        && sig.safety().is_unsafe()\n                    {\n                        let mut collector = InferVarCollector {\n                            value: (ex.hir_id, ex.span, UnsafeUseReason::Call),\n                            res: self.res,\n                        };\n\n                        // Try collecting generic arguments of the function.\n                        // Note that we do this below for any paths (that don't have to be called),\n                        // but there we do it with a different span/reason.\n                        // This takes priority.\n                        typeck_results\n                            .node_args(func.hir_id)\n                            .types()\n                            .for_each(|t| t.visit_with(&mut collector));\n\n                        // Also check the return type, for cases like `returns_unsafe_fn_ptr()()`\n                        sig.output().visit_with(&mut collector);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                // Check paths which refer to functions.\n                // We do this, instead of only checking `Call` to make sure the lint can't be\n                // avoided by storing unsafe function in a variable.\n                hir::ExprKind::Path(_) => {\n                    let ty = typeck_results.expr_ty(ex);\n\n                    // If this path refers to an unsafe function, collect inference variables which may affect it.\n                    // `is_fn` excludes closures, but those can't be unsafe.\n                    if ty.is_fn()\n                        && let sig = ty.fn_sig(self.fcx.tcx)\n                        && sig.safety().is_unsafe()\n                    {\n                        let mut collector = InferVarCollector {\n                            value: (ex.hir_id, ex.span, UnsafeUseReason::Path),\n                            res: self.res,\n                        };\n\n                        // Collect generic arguments of the function\n                        typeck_results\n                            .node_args(ex.hir_id)\n                            .types()\n                            .for_each(|t| t.visit_with(&mut collector));\n                    }\n                }\n\n                hir::ExprKind::Unary(hir::UnOp::Deref, pointer) => {\n                    if let ty::RawPtr(pointee, _) = typeck_results.expr_ty(pointer).kind() {\n                        pointee.visit_with(&mut InferVarCollector {\n                            value: (ex.hir_id, ex.span, UnsafeUseReason::Deref),\n                            res: self.res,\n                        });\n                    }\n                }\n\n                hir::ExprKind::Field(base, _) => {\n                    let base_ty = typeck_results.expr_ty(base);\n\n                    if base_ty.is_union() {\n                        typeck_results.expr_ty(ex).visit_with(&mut InferVarCollector {\n                            value: (ex.hir_id, ex.span, UnsafeUseReason::UnionField),\n                            res: self.res,\n                        });\n                    }\n                }\n\n                _ => (),\n            };\n\n            hir::intravisit::walk_expr(self, ex);\n        }\n    }\n\n    struct InferVarCollector<'r, V> {\n        value: V,\n        res: &'r mut UnordMap<ty::TyVid, V>,\n    }\n\n    impl<'tcx, V: Copy> ty::TypeVisitor<TyCtxt<'tcx>> for InferVarCollector<'_, V> {\n        fn visit_ty(&mut self, t: Ty<'tcx>) {\n            if let Some(vid) = t.ty_vid() {\n                _ = self.res.try_insert(vid, self.value);\n            } else {\n                t.super_visit_with(self)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    UnsafeInferVarsVisitor { fcx, res: &mut res }.visit_expr(&body.value);\n\n    debug!(?res, \"collected the following unsafe vars for {body_id:?}\");\n\n    res\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":350,"oldLines":8,"newStart":350,"newLines":8,"lines":["                 // coercion and subtyping.","                 let (a, b) = match    }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::TermKind<'tcx> {\n    type T = crate::ty::TermKind;\n\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: ty::TermKind::Const(cnst) => {\n                let cnst = cnst.stable(tables, cx);\n                TermKind::Const(cnst)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for crate::ty::TyKind;\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        match self {\n            ty::Bool => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Bool),\n            ty::Char => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Char),\n            ty::Int(int_ty) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Int(int_ty.stable(tables, cx))),\n            ty::Uint(uint_ty) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Uint(uint_ty.stable(tables, cx))),\n            ty::Float(float_ty) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Float(float_ty.stable(tables, cx))),\n            ty::Adt(adt_def, generic_args) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(\n                tables.adt_def(adt_def.did()),\n                generic_args.stable(tables, cx),\n            )),\n            ty::Foreign(def_id) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Foreign(tables.foreign_def(*def_id))),\n            ty::Str => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Str),\n            ty::Array(ty, constant) => {\n                TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Array(ty.stable(tables, cx), constant.stable(tables, cx)))\n            }\n            ty::Pat(ty, pat) => {\n                TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Pat(ty.stable(tables, cx), pat.stable(tables, cx)))\n            }\n            ty::Slice(ty) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Slice(ty.stable(tables, cx))),\n            ty::RawPtr(ty, mutbl) => {\n                TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::RawPtr(ty.stable(tables, cx), mutbl.stable(tables, cx)))\n            }\n            ty::Ref(region, ty, mutbl) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Ref(\n                region.stable(tables, cx),\n                ty.stable(tables, cx),\n                mutbl.stable(tables, cx),\n            )),\n            ty::FnDef(def_id, generic_args) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnDef(\n                tables.fn_def(*def_id),\n                generic_args.stable(tables, cx),\n            )),\n            ty::FnPtr(sig_tys, hdr) => {\n                TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnPtr(sig_tys.with(*hdr).stable(tables, cx)))\n            }\n            // FIXME(unsafe_binders):\n            ty::UnsafeBinder(_) => todo!(),\n            ty::Dynamic(existential_predicates, region) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Dynamic(\n                existential_predicates\n                    .iter()\n                    .map(|existential_predicate| existential_predicate.stable(tables, cx))\n                    .collect(),\n                region.stable(tables, cx),\n            )),\n            ty::Closure(def_id, generic_args) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Closure(\n                tables.closure_def(*def_id),\n                generic_args.stable(tables, cx),\n            )),\n            ty::CoroutineClosure(..) => todo!(\"FIXME(async_closures): Lower these to SMIR\"),\n            ty::Coroutine(def_id, generic_args) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Coroutine(\n                tables.coroutine_def(*def_id),\n                generic_args.stable(tables, cx),\n            )),\n            ty::Never => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Never),\n            ty::Tuple(fields) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Tuple(\n                fields.iter().map(|ty| ty.stable(tables, cx)).collect(),\n            )),\n            ty::Alias(alias_kind, alias_ty) => {\n                TyKind::Alias(alias_kind.stable(tables, cx), alias_ty.stable(tables, cx))\n            }\n            ty::Param(param_ty) => TyKind::Param(param_ty.stable(tables, cx)),\n            ty::Bound(ty::BoundVarIndexKind::Canonical, _) => {\n                unreachable!()\n            }\n            ty::Bound(ty::BoundVarIndexKind::Bound(debruijn_idx), bound_ty) => {\n                TyKind::Bound(debruijn_idx.as_usize(), bound_ty.stable(tables, cx))\n            }\n            ty::CoroutineWitness(def_id, args) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::CoroutineWitness(\n                tables.coroutine_witness_def(*def_id),\n                args.stable(tables, cx),\n            )),\n            ty::Placeholder(..) | ty::Infer(_) | ty::Error(_) => {\n                unreachable!();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::Pattern<'tcx> {\n    type T = crate::ty::Pattern;\n\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        match **self {\n            ty::PatternKind::Range { start, end } => crate::ty::Pattern::Range {\n                // FIXME(SMIR): update data structures to not have an Option here anymore\n                start: Some(start.stable(tables, cx)),\n                end: Some(end.stable(tables, cx)),\n                include_end: true,\n            },\n            ty::PatternKind::NotNull => todo!(),\n            ty::PatternKind::Or(_) => todo!(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::Const<'tcx> {\n    type T = crate::ty::TyConst;\n\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        let ct = cx.lift(*self).unwrap();\n        let kind = match ct.kind() {\n            ty::ConstKind::Value(cv) => {\n                let const_val = cx.valtree_to_const_val(cv);\n                if matches!(const_val, mir::ConstValue::ZeroSized) {\n                    crate::ty::TyConstKind::ZSTValue(cv.ty.stable(tables, cx))\n                } else {\n                    crate::ty::TyConstKind::Value(\n                        cv.ty.stable(tables, cx),\n                        alloc::new_allocation(cv.ty, const_val, tables, cx),\n                    )\n                }\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Param(param) => crate::ty::TyConstKind::Param(param.stable(tables, cx)),\n            ty::ConstKind::Unevaluated(uv) => crate::ty::TyConstKind::Unevaluated(\n                tables.const_def(uv.def),\n                uv.args.stable(tables, cx),\n            ),\n            ty::ConstKind::Error(_) => unreachable!(),\n            ty::ConstKind::Infer(_) => unreachable!(),\n            ty::ConstKind::Bound(_, _) => unimplemented!(),\n            ty::ConstKind::Placeholder(_) => unimplemented!(),\n            ty::ConstKind::Expr(_) => unimplemented!(),\n        };\n        let id = tables.intern_ty_const(ct);\n        crate::ty::TyConst::new(kind, id)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::ParamConst {\n    type T = crate::ty::ParamConst;\n    fn stable(&self, _: &mut Tables<'_, BridgeTys>, _: &CompilerCtxt<'_, BridgeTys>) -> Self::T {\n        use crate::ty::ParamConst;\n        ParamConst { index: self.index, name: self.name.to_string() }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for       has_auto_impl: self.has_auto_impl,\n            is_marker: self.is_marker,\n            is_coinductive: self.is_coinductive,\n            skip_array_during_method_dispatch: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        use crate::ty::Generics;\n\n        let params: Vec<_> = self.own_params.iter().map(|param| param.stable(tables, cx)).collect();\n        let param_def_id_to_index =\n            params.iter().map(|param| (param.def_id, param.index)).collect();\n\n        Generics {\n            parent: self.parent.map(|did| tables.generic_def(did)),\n            parent_count: self.parent_count,\n            params,\n            param_def_id_to_index,\n            has_self: self.has_self,\n            has_late_bound_regions: self\n                .has_late_bound_regions\n                .as_ref()\n                .map(|late_bound_regions| late_bound_regions.stable(tables, cx)),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for GenericParamDefKind::Const { has_default }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for rustc_middle::ty::GenericParamDef {\n    type T = crate::ty::GenericParamDef;\n\n    fn cx),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::PredicateKind<'tcx> {\n    type T = crate::ty::PredicateKind;\n\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        use rustc_middle::ty::PredicateKind;\n        match self {\n            PredicateKind::Clause(clause_kind) => {\n                crate::ty::PredicateKind::Clause(clause_kind.stable(tables, cx))\n            }\n            PredicateKind::DynCompatible(did) => {\n                crate::ty::PredicateKind::DynCompatible(tables.trait_def(*did))\n            }\n            PredicateKind::Subtype(subtype_predicate) => {\n                crate::ty::PredicateKind::SubType(subtype_predicate.stable(tables, cx))\n            }\n            PredicateKind::Coerce(coerce_predicate) => {\n                crate::ty::PredicateKind::Coerce(coerce_predicate.stable(tables, cx))\n            }\n            PredicateKind::ConstEquate(a, b) => {\n                crate::ty::PredicateKind::ConstEquate(a.stable(tables, cx), b.stable(tables, cx))\n            }\n            PredicateKind::Ambiguous => crate::ty::PredicateKind::Ambiguous,\n            PredicateKind::NormalizesTo(_pred) => unimplemented!(),\n            PredicateKind::AliasRelate(a, b, alias_relation_direction) => {\n                crate::ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                    a.kind().stable(tables, cx),\n                    b.kind().stable(tables, cx),\n                    alias_relation_direction.stable(tables, cx),\n                )\n            }\n        }\n TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(def, _)) if def.kind() == AdtKind::Union)\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_adt(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(..)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_ref(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Ref(..)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_fn(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnDef(..)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_fn_ptr(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnPtr(..)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_primitive(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(\n            self,\n            TyKind::RigidTy(\n                RigidTy::Bool\n                    | RigidTy::Char\n                    | RigidTy::Int(_)\n                    | RigidTy::Uint(_)\n                    | RigidTy::Float(_)\n            )\n        )\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_float(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Float(_)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_integral(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Int(_) | RigidTy::Uint(_)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_numeric(&self) -> bool {\n        self.is_integral() || self.is_float()\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_signed(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Int(_)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_str(&self) -> bool {\n        *self == TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Str)\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_cstr(&self) -> bool {\n        let TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(def, _)) = self else {\n            return false;\n        };\n        with(|cx| cx.adt_is_cstr(*def))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_slice(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Slice(_)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_array(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Array(..)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_mutable_ptr(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(\n            self,\n            TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::RawPtr(_, Mutability::Mut))\n                | TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Ref(_, _, Mutability::Mut))\n        )\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_raw_ptr(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::RawPtr(..)))\n    }\n\n    /// Tests if this is any kind of primitive pointer type (reference, raw pointer, fn pointer).\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_any_ptr(&self) -> bool {\n        self.is_ref() || self.is_raw_ptr() || self.is_fn_ptr()\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_coroutine(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Coroutine(..)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_closure(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Closure(..)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_box(&self) -> bool {\n        match self {\n            TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(def, _)) => def.is_box(),\n            _ => false,\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_simd(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(def, _)) if def.is_simd())\n    }\n\n    pub fn trait_principal(&self) -> Option<Binder<ExistentialTraitRef>> {\n        if let TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Dynamic(predicates, _)) = self {\n            if let Some(Binder { value: ExistentialPredicate::Trait(trait_ref), bound_vars }) =\n                predicates.first()\n            {\n                Some(Binder { value: trait_ref.clone(), bound_vars: bound_vars.clone() })\n            } else {\n                None\n            }\n        } else {\n            None\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the type of `ty[i]` for builtin types.\n    pub fn builtin_index(&self) -> Option<Ty> {\n        match self.rigid()? {\n            RigidTy::Array(ty, _) | RigidTy::Slice(ty) => Some(*ty),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the type and mutability of `*ty` for builtin types.\n    ///\n    /// The parameter `explicit` indicates if this is an *explicit* dereference.\n    /// Some types -- notably raw ptrs -- can only be dereferenced explicitly.\n    pub fn builtin_deref(&self, explicit: bool) -> Option<TypeAndMut> {\n        match self.rigid()? {\n            RigidTy::Adt(def, args) if def.is_box() => {\n                Some(TypeAndMut { ty: *args.0.first()?.ty()?, mutability: Mutability::Not })\n            }\n            RigidTy::Ref(_, ty, mutability) => {\n                Some(TypeAndMut { ty: *ty, mutability: *mutability })\n            }\n            RigidTy::RawPtr(ty, mutability) if explicit => {\n                Some(TypeAndMut { ty: *ty, mutability: *mutability })\n            }\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the function signature for function like types (Fn, FnPtr, and Closure)\n    pub fn fn_sig(&self) -> Option<PolyFnSig> {\n        match self {\n            TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnDef(def, args)) => Some(with(|cx| cx.fn_sig(*def, args))),\n            TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnPtr(sig)) => Some(sig.clone()),\n            TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Closure(_def, args)) => Some(with(|cx| cx.closure_sig(args))),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the discriminant type for this type.\n    pub fn discriminant_ty(&self) -> Option<Ty> {\n        self.rigid().map(|ty| with(|cx| cx.rigid_ty_discriminant_ty(ty)))\n    }\n\n    /// Deconstruct a function type if this is one.\n    pub fn fn_def(&self) -> Option<(FnDef, &GenericArgs)> {\n        if let TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnDef(def, args)) = self {\n            Some((*def, args))\n        } else {\n            None\n        }\n    }\n}\n\npub struct TypeAndMut {\n    pub ty: Ty,\n    pub mutability: Mutability,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub enum RigidTy {\n    Bool,\n    Char,\n    Int(IntTy),\n    Uint(UintTy),\n    Float(FloatTy),\n    Adt(AdtDef, GenericArgs),\n    Foreign(ForeignDef),\n    Str,\n    Array(Ty, TyConst),\n    Pat(Ty, Pattern),\n    Slice(Ty),\n    RawPtr(Ty, Mutability),\n    Ref(Region, Ty, Mutability),\n    FnDef(FnDef, GenericArgs),\n    FnPtr(PolyFnSig),\n    Closure(ClosureDef, GenericArgs),\n    Coroutine(CoroutineDef, GenericArgs),\n    CoroutineClosure(CoroutineClosureDef, GenericArgs),\n    Dynamic(Vec<Binder<ExistentialPredicate>>, Region),\n    Never,\n    Tuple(Vec<Ty>),\n    CoroutineWitness(CoroutineWitnessDef, GenericArgs),\n}\n\nimpl RigidTy {\n    /// Get the discriminant type for this type.\n    pub fn discriminant_ty(&self) -> Ty {\n        with(|cx| cx.rigid_ty_discriminant_ty(self))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From<RigidTy> for TyKind {\n    fn from(value: RigidTy) -> Self {\n        TyKind::RigidTy(value)\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize)]\npub enum IntTy {\n    Isize,\n    I8,\n    I16,\n    I32,\n    I64,\n    I128,\n}\n\nimpl IntTy {\n    pub fn num_bytes(self) -> usize {\n        match self {\n            IntTy::Isize => MachineInfo::target_pointer_width().bytes(),\n            IntTy::I8 => 1,\n            IntTy::I16 => 2,\n            IntTy::I32 => 4,\n            IntTy::I64 => 8,\n            IntTy::I128 => 16,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize)]\npub enum UintTy {\n    Usize,\n    U8,\n    U16,\n    U32,\n    U64,\n    U128,\n}\n\nimpl UintTy {\n    pub fn num_bytes(self) -> usize {\n        match self {\n            UintTy::Usize => MachineInfo::target_pointer_width().bytes(),\n            UintTy::U8 => 1,\n            UintTy::U16 => 2,\n            UintTy::U32 => 4,\n            UintTy::U64 => 8,\n            UintTy::U128 => 16,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize)]\npub enum FloatTy {\n    F16,\n    F32,\n    F64,\n    F128,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize)]\npub enum Movability {\n    Static,\n    Movable,\n}\n\ncrate_def! {\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n Type(Ty),\n}\n\ncrate_def_with_ty! {\n    /// Hold information about a function definition in a crate.\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    pub FnDef;\n}\n\nimpl FnDef {\n    // Get the function body if has_body(&self) -> bool {\n        with(|ctx| ctx.has_body(self.0))\n    }\n\n    /// Get the information of the intrinsic if this function is a definition of one.\n    pub fn as_intrinsic(&self) -> self.as_intrinsic().is_some()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the function signature for this function definition.\n    pub fn fn_sig(&self) -> PolyFnSig {\n        let kind = self.ty().kind();\n        kind.fn_sig().unwrap()\n    }\n}\n\ncrate_def_with_ty! {\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    pub IntrinsicDef;\n}\n\nimpl IntrinsicDef {\n    /// Returns the plain name of the intrinsic.\n    /// e.g., {\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    pub AdtDef;\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Debug, Hash, Serialize)]\npub enum AdtKind {\n    Enum,\n    Union,\n    Struct,\n}\n\nimpl AdtDef {\n    pub fn kind(&self) -> AdtKind {\n        with(|cx| cx.adt_kind(*self))\n    }\n\n    /// Retrieve the type of this Adt.\n    pub fn ty(&self) -> Ty {\n        with(|cx| cx.def_ty(self.0))\n    }\n\n    ///  pub fn is_simd(&self) -> bool {\n        with(|cx| cx.adt_is_simd(*self))\n    }\n\n    /// The number of variants in this ADT.\n    pub fn num_variants(&self) -> usize {\n        with(|cx| cx.adt_variants_len(*self))\n    }\n\n    /// Retrieve the variants in this ADT.\n    pub fn variants(&self) -> Vec<VariantDef> {\n        self.variants_iter().collect()\n    }\n\n    /// Iterate over the variants in this ADT.\n    pub fn variants_iter(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = VariantDef> {\n        (0..self.num_variants())\n            .map(|idx| VariantDef { idx: VariantIdx::to_val(idx), adt_def: *self })\n    }\n\n    pub fn variant(&self, idx: VariantIdx) -> Option<VariantDef> {\n        (idx.to_index() < self.num_variants()).then_some(VariantDef { idx, adt_def: *self })\n    }\n\n    pub fn repr(&self) -> ReprOptions {\n        with(|cx| cx.adt_repr(*self))\n    }\n\n    pub fn discriminant_for_variant(&self, idx: VariantIdx) -> Discr {\n        with(|cx| cx.adt_discr_for_variant(*self, idx))\n    }\n}\n\npub struct Discr {\n    pub val: u128,\n    pub ty: Ty,\n}\n\n/// Definition of a variant, which can be either a struct / union field or an enum variant.\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub struct VariantDef {\n    /// The variant index.\n    ///\n    /// ## Warning\n    /// Do not access this field directly!\n    pub idx: VariantIdx,\n    /// The data type where this variant comes from.\n    /// For now, we use this to retrieve information about the variant itself so we don't need to\n    /// cache more information.\n    ///\n    /// ## Warning\n    /// Do not access this field directly!\n    pub adt_def: AdtDef,\n}\n\nimpl VariantDef {\n    pub fn name(&self) -> Symbol {\n        with(|cx| cx.variant_name(*self))\n    }\n\n    /// Retrieve all the fields in this variant.\n    // We expect user to cache this and use it directly since today it is expensive to generate all\n    // fields name.\n    pub fn fields(&self) -> Vec<FieldDef> {\n        with(|cx| cx.variant_fields(*self))\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub struct FieldDef {\n    /// The field definition.\n    ///\n    /// ## Warning\n    /// Do not access this field directly! This is public for the compiler to have access to it.\n    pub def: DefId,\n\n    /// The field name.\n    pub name: Symbol,\n}\n\nimpl FieldDef {\n    /// Retrieve the type of this field instantiating and normalizing it with the given arguments.\n    ///\n    /// This will assume the type can be instantiated with these arguments.\n    pub fn ty_with_args(&self, args: &GenericArgs) -> Ty {\n        with(|cx| cx.def_ty_with_args(self.def, args))\n    }\n\n    /// Retrieve the type of this field.\n    pub fn ty(&self) -> Ty {\n        with(|cx| cx.def_ty(self.def))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Display for AdtKind {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        f.write_str(match self {\n            AdtKind::Enum => \"enum\",\n            AdtKind::Union => \"union\",\n            AdtKind::Struct => \"struct\",\n        })\n    }\n}\n\nimpl AdtKind {\n    pub fn is_enum(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, AdtKind::Enum)\n    }\n\n    pub fn is_struct(&self) -> GenericArgs(pub Vec<GenericArgKind>);\n\nimpl std::ops::Index<ParamTy> for GenericArgs {\n    type Output = Ty;\n\n    fn index(&self, index: ParamTy) -> &Self::Output {\n        self.0[index.index as usize].expect_ty()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::ops::Index<ParamConst> for GenericArgs {\n    type Output = TyConst;\n\n    fn index(&self, index: ParamConst) -> &Self::Output {\n        self.0[index.index as usize].expect_const()\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub enum GenericArgKind {\n    Lifetime(Region),\n    Type(Ty),\n    Const(TyConst),\n}\n\nimpl GenericArgKind {\n    /// Panic if this generic argument is not a type, otherwise\n    /// return the type.\n    #[track_caller]\n    pub fn expect_ty(&self) -> &Ty {\n        match self {\n            GenericArgKind::Type(ty) => ty,\n            _ => panic!(\"{self:?}\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Panic if this generic argument is not a const, otherwise\n    /// return the const.\n    #[track_caller]\n    pub fn expect_const(&self) -> &TyConst {\n        match self {\n            GenericArgKind::Const(c) => c,\n            _ => panic!(\"{self:?}\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Return the generic argument type if applicable, otherwise return `None`.\n    pub fn ty(&self) -> Option<&Ty> {\n        match self {\n            GenericArgKind::Type(ty) => Some(ty),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub enum TermKind {\n    Type(Ty),\n    Const(TyConst),\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub PolyFnSig {\n    /// Compute a `FnAbi` suitable for indirect calls, i.e. to `fn` pointers.\n    ///\n    /// NB: this doesn't handle virtual calls - those should use `Instance::fn_abi`\n    /// Ty {\n        self.inputs_and_output[self.inputs_and_output.len() - 1]\n    }\n\n    pub fn inputs(&self) -> &[Ty] {\n        &self.inputs_and_output[..self.inputs_and_output.len() - 1]\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Debug, Serialize)]\npub enum Abi {\n    Rust,\n    C { unwind: bool },\n    Cdecl { unwind: bool },\n    Stdcall { unwind: bool },\n    Fastcall { unwind: bool },\n    Vectorcall { unwind: bool },\n    Thiscall { unwind: bool },\n    Aapcs { unwind: bool },\n    Win64 { unwind: bool },\n    SysV64 { unwind: bool },\n    PtxKernel,\n    Msp430Interrupt,\n    X86Interrupt,\n    GpuKernel,\n    EfiApi,\n    AvrInterrupt,\n    AvrNonBlockingInterrupt,\n    CCmseNonSecureCall,\n    CCmseNonSecureEntry,\n    System { unwind: bool },\n    RustCall,\n    Unadjusted,\n    RustCold,\n    RiscvInterruptM,\n    RiscvInterruptS,\n    RustInvalid,\n    Custom,\n}\n\n/// A binder represents a possibly generic type and its bound vars.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub struct Binder<T> {\n    pub value: T,\n    pub bound_vars: Vec<BoundVariableKind>,\n}\n\nimpl<T> Binder<T> {\n    /// Create a new binder with the given bound vars.\n    pub fn bind_with_vars(value: T, bound_vars: Vec<BoundVariableKind>) -> Self {\n        Binder { value, bound_vars }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new binder with no bounded variable.\n    pub fn dummy(value: T) -> Self {\n        Binder { value, bound_vars: vec![] }\n    }\n\n    pub fn skip_binder(self) -> T {\n        self.value\n    }\n\n    pub fn map_bound_ref<F, U>(&self, f: F) -> Binder<U>\n    where\n        F: FnOnce(&T) -> U,\n    {\n        let Binder { value, bound_vars } = self;\n        let new_value = f(value);\n        Binder { value: new_value, bound_vars: bound_vars.clone() }\n    }\n\n    pub fn map_bound<F, U>(self, f: F) -> Binder<U>\n    where\n        F: FnOnce(T) -> U,\n    {\n        let Binder { value, bound_vars } = self;\n        let new_value = f(value);\n        Binder { value: new_value, bound_vars }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub struct EarlyBinder<T> {\n    pub value: T,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub enum BoundVariableKind {\n    Ty(BoundTyKind),\n    Region(BoundRegionKind),\n    Const,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Debug, BrNamed(BrNamedDef, String),\n    BrEnv,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub enum ExistentialPredicate {\n    Trait(ExistentialTraitRef),\n    Projection(ExistentialProjection),\n    AutoTrait(TraitDef),\n}\n\n/// An existential reference to a trait where `Self` is not included.\n///\n/// The `generic_args` will include any other known argument.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub struct ExistentialTraitRef {\n    pub def_id: TraitDef,\n    pub generic_args: GenericArgs,\n}\n\nimpl Binder<ExistentialTraitRef> {\n    pub fn with_self_ty(&self, self_ty: Ty) -> Binder<TraitRef> {\n        self.map_bound_ref(|trait_ref| trait_ref.with_self_ty(self_ty))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl ExistentialTraitRef {\n    pub fn with_self_ty(&self, self_ty: Ty) -> TraitRef {\n        TraitRef::new(self.def_id, self_ty, &self.generic_args)\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub struct ExistentialProjection {\n    pub def_id: TraitDef,\n    pub generic_args: GenericArgs,\n    pub term: TermKind,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub struct ParamTy {\n    pub Vec<Option<u8>>;\n\n/// Size in bytes.\npub type Size = usize;\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Debug, Hash, Serialize)]\npub struct Prov(pub AllocId);\n\npub type Align = u64;\npub type Two entries in this map are always at least a pointer size apart.\n    pub ptrs: Vec<(Size, Prov)>,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub struct Allocation {\n    pub bytes: Bytes,\n    pub provenance: ProvenanceMap,\n    pub align: Align,\n    pub mutability: Mutability,\n}\n\nimpl Allocation {\n    /// Get a vector of bytes for an Allocation that has been fully initialized\n    pub fn raw_bytes(&self) -> Result<Vec<u8>, Error> {\n        self.bytes\n            .iter()\n            .copied()\n            .collect::<Option<Vec<_>>>()\n            .ok_or_else(|| error!(\"Found uninitialized bytes: `{:?}`\", self.bytes))\n    }\n\n    /// Read a uint value from the specified range.\n    pub fn read_partial_uint(&self, range: Range<usize>) -> Result<u128, Error> {\n        if range.end - range.start > 16 {\n            return Err(error!(\"Allocation is bigger than largest integer\"));\n        }\n        if range.end > self.bytes.len() {\n            return Err(error!(\n                \"Range is out of bounds. Allocation length is `{}`, but requested range `{:?}`\",\n                self.bytes.len(),\n                range\n            ));\n        }\n        let raw = self.bytes[range]\n            .iter()\n            .copied()\n            .collect::<Option<Vec<_>>>()\n            .ok_or_else(|| error!(\"Found uninitialized bytes: `{:?}`\", self.bytes))?;\n        read_target_uint(&raw)\n    }\n\n    /// Read this allocation and try to convert it to an unassigned integer.\n    pub fn read_uint(&self) -> Result<u128, Error> {\n        if self.bytes.len() > 16 {\n            return Err(error!(\"Allocation is bigger than largest integer\"));\n        }\n        let raw = self.raw_bytes()?;\n        read_target_uint(&raw)\n    }\n\n    /// Read this allocation and try to convert it to a signed integer.\n    pub fn read_int(&self) -> Result<i128, Error> {\n        if self.bytes.len() > 16 {\n            return Err(error!(\"Allocation is bigger than largest integer\"));\n        }\n        let raw = self.raw_bytes()?;\n        read_target_int(&raw)\n    }\n\n    /// Read this allocation and try to convert it to a boolean.\n    pub fn read_bool(&self) -> Result<bool, Error> {\n        match self.read_int()? {\n            0 => Ok(false),\n            1 => Ok(true),\n            val => Err(error!(\"Unexpected value for bool: `{val}`\")),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Read this allocation as a pointer and return whether it represents a `null` pointer.\n    pub fn is_null(&self) -> Result<bool, Error> {\n        let len = self.bytes.len();\n        let ptr_len = MachineInfo::target_pointer_width().bytes();\n        if len != ptr_len {\n            return Err(error!(\"Expected width of pointer (`{ptr_len}`), but found: `{len}`\"));\n        }\n        Ok(self.read_uint()? == 0 && self.provenance.ptrs.is_empty())\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub enum ConstantKind {\n    Ty(TyConst),\n Ty::from_rigid_kind(RigidTy::Uint(inner))\n    }\n\n    /// Get a type layout.\n    pub fn layout(self) -> Result<Layout, Error> {\n        with(|cx| cx.ty_layout(self))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Ty {\n    pub fn kind(&self) -> TyKind {\n        with(|context| context.ty_kind(*self))\n    }\n}\n\n/// Represents a pattern in the type system\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub enum Pattern {\n    Range { start: Option<TyConst>, end: Option<TyConst>, include_end: bool },\n}\n\n/// Represents a constant in the type system\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub struct TyConst {\n    pub(crate) kind: TyConstKind,\n    pub id: TyConstId,\n}\n\nimpl TyConst {\n    pub fn new(kind: TyConstKind, id: TyConstId) -> TyConst {\n        Self { kind, id }\n    }\n\n    /// Retrieve the constant kind.\n    pub fn kind(&self) -> &TyConstKind {\n        &self.kind\n    }\n\n    /// Creates an interned usize constant.\n    pub fn try_from_target_usize(val: u64) -> Result<Self, Error> {\n        with(|cx| cx.try_new_ty_const_uint(val.into(), UintTy::Usize))\n    }\n\n    /// Try to evaluate to a target `usize`.\n    pub fn eval_target_usize(&self) -> Result<u64, Error> {\n        with(|cx| cx.eval_target_usize_ty(self))\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub enum TyConstKind {\n    Param(ParamConst),\n    Bound(DebruijnIndex, BoundVar),\n    Unevaluated(ConstDef, GenericArgs),\n\n    // FIXME: These should be a valtree\n    Value(Ty, Allocation),\n    ZSTValue(Ty),\n}\n\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash)]\npub struct TyConstId(usize, ThreadLocalIndex);\n\n/// Represents a constant in MIR\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub struct MirConst {\n    /// The constant kind.\n    pub(crate) kind: ConstantKind,\n    /// The constant type.\n    pub(crate) ty: Ty,\n    /// Used for internal tracking of the internal constant.\n    pub id: MirConstId,\n}\n\nimpl MirConst {\n    /// Build a constant. Note that this should only be used by the compiler.\n    pub fn new(kind: ConstantKind, ty: Ty, id: MirConstId) -> MirConst {\n        MirConst { kind, ty, id }\n    }\n\n    /// Retrieve the constant kind.\n    pub fn kind(&self) -> &ConstantKind {\n        &self.kind\n    }\n\n    /// Get the constant type.\n    pub fn ty(&self) -> Ty {\n        self.ty\n    }\n\n    /// Try to evaluate to a target `usize`.\n    pub fn eval_target_usize(&self) -> Result<u64, Error> {\n        with(|cx| cx.eval_target_usize(self))\n    }\n\n    /// Create a constant that represents a new zero-sized constant of type T.\n    /// Fails if the type is not a ZST or if it doesn't have a known size.\n    pub fn try_new_zero_sized(ty: Ty) -> Result<MirConst, Error> {\n        with(|cx| cx.try_new_const_zst(ty))\n    }\n\n    /// Build a new constant that represents the given string.\n    ///\n    /// Note that there is no guarantee today about duplication of the same constant.\n    /// I.e.: Calling this function multiple times with the same argument may or may not return\n    /// the same allocation.\n    pub fn from_str(value: &str) -> MirConst {\n        with(|cx| cx.new_const_str(value))\n    }\n\n    /// Build a new constant that represents the given boolean value.\n    pub fn from_bool(value: bool) -> MirConst {\n        with(|cx| cx.new_const_bool(value))\n    }\n\n    /// Build a new constant that represents the given unsigned integer.\n    pub fn try_from_uint(value: u128, uint_ty: UintTy) -> Result<MirConst, Error> {\n        with(|cx| cx.try_new_const_uint(value, uint_ty))\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub struct MirConstId(usize, ThreadLocalIndex);\n\ntype Ident = Opaque;\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub struct Region {\n    pub kind: RegionKind,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub enum RegionKind {\n    ReEarlyParam(EarlyParamRegion),\n    ReBound(DebruijnIndex, BoundRegion),\n    ReStatic,\n    RePlaceholder(Placeholder<BoundRegion>),\n    ReErased,\n}\n\npub(crate) type DebruijnIndex = u32;\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub struct EarlyParamRegion {\n    pub index: u32,\n    pub name: Symbol,\n}\n\npub(crate) type BoundVar = u32;\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub struct BoundRegion {\n    pub var: BoundVar,\n    pub kind: BoundRegionKind,\n}\n\npub(crate) type UniverseIndex = u32;\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub struct Placeholder<T> {\n    pub universe: UniverseIndex,\n    pub bound: T,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub struct Span(usize, ThreadLocalIndex);\n\nimpl Debug for Span {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        f.debug_struct(\"Span\")\n            .field(\"id\", &self.0)\n            .field(\"repr\", &with(|cx| cx.span_to_string(*self)))\n            .finish()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Span {\n    /// Return filename for diagnostic purposes\n    pub fn get_filename(&self) -> Filename {\n        with(|c| c.get_filename(self))\n    }\n\n    /// Return lines that correspond to this `Span`\n    pub fn get_lines(&self) -> LineInfo {\n        with(|c| c.get_lines(self))\n    }\n\n    /// Return the span location to be printed in diagnostic messages.\n    ///\n    /// This may leak local file paths and should not be used to build artifacts that may be\n    /// distributed.\n    pub fn diagnostic(&self) -> String {\n        with(|c| c.span_to_string(*self))\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Serialize)]\n/// Information you get from `Span` in a struct form.\n/// Line and col start from 1.\npub struct LineInfo {\n    pub start_line: usize,\n    pub start_col: usize,\n    pub end_line: usize,\n    pub end_col: usize,\n}\n\nimpl LineInfo {\n    pub fn from(lines: (usize, usize, usize, usize)) -> Self {\n        LineInfo { start_line: lines.0, start_col: lines.1, end_line: lines.2, end_col: lines.3 }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub enum TyKind {\n    RigidTy(RigidTy),\n    Alias(AliasKind, AliasTy),\n    Param(ParamTy),\n    Bound(usize, BoundTy),\n}\n\nimpl TyKind {\n    pub fn rigid(&self) -> Option<&RigidTy> {\n        if let TyKind::RigidTy(inner) = self { Some(inner) } else { None }\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_unit(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Tuple(data)) if data.is_empty())\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_bool(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Bool))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_char(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Char))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_trait(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Dynamic(_, _)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_enum(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(def, _)) if def.kind() == AdtKind::Enum)\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_struct(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(def, _)) if def.kind() == AdtKind::Struct)\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_union(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(def, _)) if def.kind() == AdtKind::Union)\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_adt(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(..)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_ref(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Ref(..)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_fn(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnDef(..)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_fn_ptr(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnPtr(..)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_primitive(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(\n            self,\n            TyKind::RigidTy(\n                RigidTy::Bool\n                    | RigidTy::Char\n                    | RigidTy::Int(_)\n                    | RigidTy::Uint(_)\n                    | RigidTy::Float(_)\n            )\n        )\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_float(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Float(_)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_integral(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Int(_) | RigidTy::Uint(_)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_numeric(&self) -> bool {\n        self.is_integral() || self.is_float()\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_signed(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Int(_)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_str(&self) -> bool {\n        *self == TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Str)\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_cstr(&self) -> bool {\n        let TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(def, _)) = self else {\n            return false;\n        };\n        with(|cx| cx.adt_is_cstr(*def))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_slice(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Slice(_)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_array(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Array(..)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_mutable_ptr(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(\n            self,\n            TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::RawPtr(_, Mutability::Mut))\n                | TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Ref(_, _, Mutability::Mut))\n        )\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_raw_ptr(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::RawPtr(..)))\n    }\n\n    /// Tests if this is any kind of primitive pointer type (reference, raw pointer, fn pointer).\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_any_ptr(&self) -> bool {\n        self.is_ref() || self.is_raw_ptr() || self.is_fn_ptr()\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_coroutine(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Coroutine(..)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_closure(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Closure(..)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_box(&self) -> bool {\n        match self {\n            TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(def, _)) => def.is_box(),\n            _ => false,\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_simd(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(def, _)) if def.is_simd())\n    }\n\n    pub fn trait_principal(&self) -> Option<Binder<ExistentialTraitRef>> {\n        if let TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Dynamic(predicates, _)) = self {\n            if let Some(Binder { value: ExistentialPredicate::Trait(trait_ref), bound_vars }) =\n                predicates.first()\n            {\n                Some(Binder { value: trait_ref.clone(), bound_vars: bound_vars.clone() })\n            } else {\n                None\n            }\n        } else {\n            None\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the type of `ty[i]` for builtin types.\n    pub fn builtin_index(&self) -> Option<Ty> {\n        match self.rigid()? {\n            RigidTy::Array(ty, _) | RigidTy::Slice(ty) => Some(*ty),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the type and mutability of `*ty` for builtin types.\n    ///\n    /// The parameter `explicit` indicates if this is an *explicit* dereference.\n    /// Some types -- notably raw ptrs -- can only be dereferenced explicitly.\n    pub fn builtin_deref(&self, explicit: bool) -> Option<TypeAndMut> {\n        match self.rigid()? {\n            RigidTy::Adt(def, args) if def.is_box() => {\n                Some(TypeAndMut { ty: *args.0.first()?.ty()?, mutability: Mutability::Not })\n            }\n            RigidTy::Ref(_, ty, mutability) => {\n                Some(TypeAndMut { ty: *ty, mutability: *mutability })\n            }\n            RigidTy::RawPtr(ty, mutability) if explicit => {\n                Some(TypeAndMut { ty: *ty, mutability: *mutability })\n            }\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the function signature for function like types (Fn, FnPtr, and Closure)\n    pub fn fn_sig(&self) -> Option<PolyFnSig> {\n        match self {\n            TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnDef(def, args)) => Some(with(|cx| cx.fn_sig(*def, args))),\n            TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnPtr(sig)) => Some(sig.clone()),\n            TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Closure(_def, args)) => Some(with(|cx| cx.closure_sig(args))),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the discriminant type for this type.\n    pub fn discriminant_ty(&self) -> Option<Ty> {\n        self.rigid().map(|ty| with(|cx| cx.rigid_ty_discriminant_ty(ty)))\n    }\n\n    /// Deconstruct a function type if this is one.\n    pub fn fn_def(&self) -> Option<(FnDef, &GenericArgs)> {\n        if let TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnDef(def, args)) = self {\n            Some((*def, args))\n        } else {\n            None\n        }\n    }\n}\n\npub struct TypeAndMut {\n    pub ty: Ty,\n    pub mutability: Mutability,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub enum RigidTy {\n    Bool,\n    Char,\n    Int(IntTy),\n    Uint(UintTy),\n    Float(FloatTy),\n    Adt(AdtDef, GenericArgs),\n    Foreign(ForeignDef),\n    Str,\n    Array(Ty, TyConst),\n    Pat(Ty, Pattern),\n    Slice(Ty),\n    RawPtr(Ty, Mutability),\n    Ref(Region, Ty, Mutability),\n    FnDef(FnDef, GenericArgs),\n    FnPtr(PolyFnSig),\n    Closure(ClosureDef, GenericArgs),\n    Coroutine(CoroutineDef, GenericArgs),\n    CoroutineClosure(CoroutineClosureDef, GenericArgs),\n    Dynamic(Vec<Binder<ExistentialPredicate>>, Region),\n    Never,\n    Tuple(Vec<Ty>),\n    CoroutineWitness(CoroutineWitnessDef, GenericArgs),\n}\n\nimpl RigidTy {\n    /// Get the discriminant type for this type.\n    pub fn discriminant_ty(&self) -> Ty {\n        with(|cx| cx.rigid_ty_discriminant_ty(self))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From<RigidTy> for TyKind {\n    fn from(value: RigidTy) -> Self {\n        TyKind::RigidTy(value)\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize)]\npub enum IntTy {\n    Isize,\n    I8,\n    I16,\n    I32,\n    I64,\n    I128,\n}\n\nimpl IntTy {\n    pub fn num_bytes(self) -> usize {\n        match self {\n            IntTy::Isize => MachineInfo::target_pointer_width().bytes(),\n            IntTy::I8 => 1,\n            IntTy::I16 => 2,\n            IntTy::I32 => 4,\n            IntTy::I64 => 8,\n            IntTy::I128 => 16,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize)]\npub enum UintTy {\n    Usize,\n    U8,\n    U16,\n    U32,\n    U64,\n    U128,\n}\n\nimpl UintTy            UintTy::U32 => 4,\n            UintTy::U64 => 8,\n            UintTy::U128 => 16,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize)]\npub enum FloatTy {\n    F16,\n    F32,\n    F64,\n    F128,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize)]\npub enum Movability {\n    Static,\n    Movable,\n}\n\ncrate_def! {\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n /// Hold information about a ForeignItem in a crate.\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    pub ForeignDef;\n}\n\nimpl ForeignDef {\n    pub fn kind(&self) -> ForeignItemKind {\n        with(|cx| Type(Ty),\n}\n\ncrate_def_with_ty! {\n    /// Hold information about a function definition in a crate.\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    pub FnDef;\n}\n\nimpl FnDef {\n    // Get the function body if has_body(&self) -> bool {\n        with(|ctx| ctx.has_body(self.0))\n    }\n\n    /// Get the information of the intrinsic if this function is a definition of one.\n    pub fn as_intrinsic(&self) -> self.as_intrinsic().is_some()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the function signature for this function definition.\n    pub fn fn_sig(&self) -> PolyFnSig {\n        let kind = self.ty().kind();\n    fn from(def: IntrinsicDef) -> Self {\n        FnDef(def.0)\n    }\n}\n\ncrate_def! {\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    pub ClosureDef;\n}\n\nimpl ClosureDef {\n    /// Retrieves the body of the
